zoom\_in

This software repository provides a framework for programmatic web browser automation, allowing AI agents to interact with web pages, execute Python code within browser contexts, and manage persistent browser sessions. It enables autonomous agents to perform complex web-based tasks by leveraging advanced browser control, AI-driven decision-making, and dynamic code execution capabilities.

A browser automation core forms the foundation of the system, providing low-level control over web browser interactions through the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). This core manages browser sessions, page navigation, element interaction, and an event-driven communication system to monitor browser activity. See [Browser Automation Core](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core) for more details.

AI agents integrate with Large Language Models (LLMs) to interpret tasks, generate actions, and orchestrate complex workflows. These agents manage their internal state and conversation history, leveraging LLMs for informed decision-making and structured output. A unified interface supports various LLM providers, ensuring flexible integration. See [AI Agent Integration and Orchestration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#ai-agent-integration-and-orchestration) for more details.

Dynamic Python code executes within the browser context, resembling a notebook environment. This enables agents to generate and run Python code for browser control, access a persistent namespace, and utilize data science libraries for iterative task completion. A type-safe sandboxing mechanism ensures secure remote execution of these functions, streaming real-time events. See [Code Execution and Sandboxing](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#code-execution-and-sandboxing) for more details.

Browser Document Object Model (DOM) data processing creates an enhanced, actionable representation of web pages. This includes determining element visibility, clickability, and layout. It also extracts clean, structured markdown from browser content and provides tools for analyzing DOM structure and generating robust CSS selectors. See [DOM Processing and Content Extraction](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#dom-processing-and-content-extraction) for more details.

A virtual file system manages in-memory file objects that asynchronously persist to disk. This supports various file types, including intelligent handling for PDFs and DOCX files, and incorporates robust filename management. External integrations, such as with the Gmail API, allow agents to read emails and extract information. See [Virtual File System and Integrations](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#virtual-file-system-and-integrations) for more details.

The software integrates with the Model Context Protocol (MCP), acting as both a client to discover and register external tools, and a server to expose its browser automation capabilities to other MCP clients. See [Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#model-context-protocol-mcp-integration) for more details.

A command-line interface (CLI) offers persistent browser automation, including navigation, element interaction, JavaScript execution, and data extraction. The CLI supports various browser modes and profile management, and allows Python scripting within active browser sessions. See [Command-Line Interface (CLI) for Browser Automation](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#command-line-interface-cli-for-browser-automation) for more details.

Practical applications and illustrative examples demonstrate the library's capabilities across diverse scenarios, including web automation with AI models, advanced browser configurations, cloud API interactions, custom tool development, and real-world use cases. See [Examples and Use Cases](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#examples-and-use-cases) for more details.

zoom\_in

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) repository provides a structured environment for development, contribution, and code quality maintenance. New contributors can set up their local environment, understand contribution guidelines, and utilize scripts for development, testing, and ensuring code quality.

Detailed guidelines for contributing to the project, including setting up a local development environment and identifying open issues, are provided in the repository's [`.github`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/test.sh#L2) directory, specifically in [`.github/CONTRIBUTING.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md). Information on reporting security vulnerabilities is also available in [`.github/SECURITY.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/.github/SECURITY.md).

For development environment setup and management, the [`bin`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin) directory contains scripts that automate tasks such as installing dependencies and activating virtual environments. The [`setup.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L1007) script, located at [`bin/setup.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/setup.sh), streamlines the initial setup process by installing the [`uv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L6) package manager, creating a virtual environment, and installing all project dependencies.

To ensure code quality and facilitate testing, a suite of scripts is available within the [`bin`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin) directory. The [`lint.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L1010) script ([`bin/lint.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/lint.sh)) performs linting, formatting, and type checking, supporting various modes for comprehensive or quick checks. The [`test.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L1013) script ([`bin/test.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/test.sh)) is used to execute project tests, particularly those run in continuous integration environments.

The project also supports Dockerized development environments, which are detailed in the [`docker`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker) directory. This approach provides reproducible and optimized development environments through various Dockerfiles. A key aspect is the building of optimized Docker base images, as described in [Building Optimized Docker Base Images](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#project-setup-and-development-workflow-dockerized-development-environments-building-optimized-docker-base-images), which includes system dependencies, Chromium, and Python dependencies to accelerate subsequent Docker builds. The [`build-base-images.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/README.md?plain=1#L9) script ([`docker/build-base-images.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/build-base-images.sh)) automates the creation and pushing of these base images.

### Contributing to the Project

link

Area

File Path

Key Instructions

Important Details

**General Contributions**

`.github/CONTRIBUTING.md`

Read Contribution Guidelines and Local Development Setup Guide. Look for `#help-wanted` issues.

Links provided for detailed guides and open issues.

**Security Reporting**

`.github/SECURITY.md`

Report vulnerabilities via Github security advisory.

**Do not** use issues, discussions, or pull requests for security. Include detailed information like issue type, affected files, reproduction steps, and impact.

To facilitate community involvement and code contributions, the project provides specific guidelines and resources. Contributors can find comprehensive instructions for setting up a local development environment and understanding the project's coding standards through the [`Contribution Guidelines`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md?plain=1#L5) link provided in [`.github/CONTRIBUTING.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md). This file also directs developers to open issues tagged [`#help-wanted`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md?plain=1#L7) on GitHub, making it easier to identify tasks suitable for new contributions.

For security-related concerns, a dedicated process is outlined in [`.github/SECURITY.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/.github/SECURITY.md). This document emphasizes private disclosure of vulnerabilities via a [`Github security advisory`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/.github/SECURITY.md?plain=1#L1) rather than public channels. It specifies the necessary information for a security report, including the type of issue, affected files, reproduction steps, and potential impact, to ensure efficient resolution.

The local development environment for the project is configured and managed through a set of shell scripts located in the [`bin`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin) directory. These scripts streamline the setup process, ensure consistent dependency management, and provide tools for maintaining code quality.

The primary setup script, [`bin/setup.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/setup.sh), automates the initial configuration of the local environment. This script installs [`uv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L6), a Python package installer, creates a dedicated virtual environment, and then installs all necessary project dependencies, including those for development and extra functionalities. It also provides instructions for activating the virtual environment and integrating with interactive Python environments like [`ipython`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/setup.sh#L49) for direct library usage. To ensure the environment variables for logging and LLM API keys are correctly set, it guides users on configuring a [`.env`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L36) file and verifies the successful installation of the project package.

After the initial setup, developers can maintain code quality using the [`bin/lint.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/lint.sh) script. This script enforces code standards by running various checks, including linting, formatting, and type checking, predominantly for Python files. It offers different modes to accommodate various development workflows: a full mode for comprehensive checks, a quick mode for faster feedback on changed files by skipping type checking, and a staged mode for pre-commit checks on files marked for commit. The script uses parallel execution for these checks and provides visual progress feedback through a spinner, along with detailed error reporting to temporary log files if any checks fail.

For validating the project's functionality, the [`bin/test.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/test.sh) script executes the core test suite. This script utilizes [`pytest`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L44) to run tests, with automatic parallelization to speed up execution, specifically targeting tests within the [`tests/ci`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L44) directory. It also allows for passing additional command-line arguments to [`pytest`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L44) for more granular control over test runs.

The project maintains code quality and ensures reliability through a set of scripts that automate linting, formatting, type checking, and testing. These scripts are designed to integrate seamlessly into the development workflow, providing options for both comprehensive and rapid checks.

The [`lint.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L1010) script, located at [`bin/lint.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/lint.sh), enforces code style and catches potential errors across Python files. It offers three modes of operation: a full mode that runs all checks on all project files, a quick mode that focuses on changed files and skips slower type checking for faster feedback, and a staged mode that processes only files staged for commit. This script utilizes tools like [`ruff check`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L50) for linting, [`ruff format`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L50) for automatic code formatting, and [`pyright`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L49) for static type checking. To minimize execution time, these checks are run in parallel, with outputs logged to temporary files for detailed error reporting. The script also includes a [`fail-fast`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/lint.sh#L7) option, allowing developers to immediately identify and address issues.

For testing, the [`test.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L1013) script, found at [`bin/test.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/bin/test.sh), executes the project's primary test suite. It leverages [`pytest`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L44) and automatically parallelizes test execution to speed up the process. This script is configured to run tests specifically within the [`tests/ci`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L44) directory, ensuring that critical continuous integration tests are always covered. It also supports passing additional arguments directly to [`pytest`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L44) for customized test runs, offering flexibility for developers to target specific tests or configurations.

These scripts are part of the broader [Project Setup and Development Workflow](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#project-setup-and-development-workflow), ensuring that contributions adhere to established quality standards and function as expected before integration.

This project utilizes Docker to establish reproducible and optimized development and deployment environments. The approach focuses on efficient image construction, leveraging multi-stage builds and pre-built base images to significantly reduce build times. The core components for this system reside in the [`docker`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker) directory.

A key aspect of this Docker strategy involves creating a set of optimized base images. These base images are designed to contain common, frequently unchanged dependencies, thereby accelerating subsequent builds. The [`build-base-images.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/README.md?plain=1#L9) script, located in [`docker/build-base-images.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/build-base-images.sh), automates the process of building and pushing these foundational images to a Docker registry. This script supports multi-platform builds using [`docker buildx`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/Dockerfile#L1), ensuring compatibility across different architectures and enabling efficient pushing of images.

The base images are structured in a layered fashion:

-   **System Dependencies**: The [`base-system`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/build-base-images.sh#L54) image, defined in [`docker/base-images/system`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/base-images/system), provides a minimal Python environment along with essential system utilities like [`ca-certificates`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/Dockerfile#L124), [`curl`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L19), and [`wget`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/Dockerfile#L124). It also integrates the [`uv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L6) package manager, which is copied from a pre-built image to enable fast dependency management.
-   **Chromium**: Building upon the [`base-system`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/build-base-images.sh#L54), the [`base-chromium`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/build-base-images.sh#L55) image (defined in [`docker/base-images/chromium`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/base-images/chromium)) includes the Chromium web browser. This is achieved by temporarily installing [`playwright`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L818) to manage Chromium installation and its dependencies, followed by a cleanup phase to minimize the final image size. This layer is crucial for any browser automation tasks.
-   **Python Dependencies**: The [`base-python-deps`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/Dockerfile.fast#L4) image, located in [`docker/base-images/python-deps`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/base-images/python-deps), extends [`base-chromium`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/build-base-images.sh#L55). It sets up a dedicated Python virtual environment and installs project-specific Python dependencies using [`uv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L6). This layer is optimized to leverage [`uv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L6)'s caching mechanisms, ensuring that Python packages are installed efficiently.

By maintaining these distinct base images, the project achieves significantly faster Docker build times for the main application, especially during iterative development. For instance, the [`Dockerfile.fast`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/README.md?plain=1#L12) file, described in [`docker/README.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/README.md), is designed to utilize these pre-built base images, allowing for builds in approximately 30 seconds, compared to the roughly 2 minutes required for a standard, self-contained [`Dockerfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/Dockerfile#L4) build described in [`docker/README.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/README.md). This modular approach to Docker image construction streamlines the development workflow and ensures consistent environments. For a more detailed breakdown of how these base images are constructed, refer to [Building Optimized Docker Base Images](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#project-setup-and-development-workflow-dockerized-development-environments-building-optimized-docker-base-images).

zoom\_in

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) project utilizes a structured approach to Docker image building to optimize development workflows and deployment. This involves creating multi-platform base images that are pre-configured with essential system dependencies, the Chromium browser, and Python dependencies. This layered approach significantly reduces subsequent build times and ensures consistent environments.

The base images are defined within the [`docker/base-images`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/base-images) directory. The build process for these images is automated by the [`docker/build-base-images.sh`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/build-base-images.sh) script, which handles the sequential construction and optional pushing to a Docker registry, supporting multi-platform architectures through [`docker buildx`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/Dockerfile#L1).

The hierarchy of base images includes:

-   **System Dependencies**: A foundational image, defined in [`docker/base-images/system/Dockerfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/base-images/system/Dockerfile), starts from [`python:3.12-slim`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/Dockerfile#L20) and installs core utilities like [`ca-certificates`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/Dockerfile#L124), [`curl`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L19), and [`wget`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/Dockerfile#L124). Crucially, it integrates the [`uv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L6) package manager by copying its executables from a pre-built image, making [`uv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L6) readily available for efficient Python dependency management.
-   **Chromium Browser**: Extending the system image, the Chromium image, defined in [`docker/base-images/chromium/Dockerfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/base-images/chromium/Dockerfile), incorporates the Chromium web browser. This is achieved by temporarily installing [`playwright`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L818) to manage the Chromium installation and its associated dependencies. After installation, [`playwright`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L818) is uninstalled to minimize the final image size, leaving only the browser and its necessary runtime components.
-   **Python Dependencies**: The final base image in the sequence, defined in [`docker/base-images/python-deps/Dockerfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docker/base-images/python-deps/Dockerfile), builds upon the Chromium-enabled image. It sets up a Python virtual environment and efficiently installs project-specific Python dependencies using [`uv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L6). This step is optimized with caching to accelerate rebuilds, ensuring that applications have their Python environment ready without redundant installations.

This tiered image construction strategy allows for rapid iteration and deployment, as main application Dockerfiles can leverage these pre-built base images, drastically cutting down build times. For more details on the overall Docker strategy, refer to [Dockerized Development Environments](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#project-setup-and-development-workflow-dockerized-development-environments).

## Browser Automation Core

zoom\_in

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) project provides foundational components for browser automation, focusing on low-level interactions with web browsers primarily through the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). This architecture allows for granular control over browser sessions, pages, and individual elements, supporting various automation tasks from simple navigation to complex AI-driven interactions.

At its core, [`CDP-Use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L73) provides a high-level abstraction similar to other browser automation libraries. It exposes key components for interacting with the browser: [`Page`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L39) for managing browser tabs or iframes, [`Element`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/element.py#L62) for interacting with specific DOM elements, and [`Mouse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/mouse.py#L12) for simulating mouse actions such as clicks, movements, and scrolling. These components are publicly exposed through [`browser_use/actor/__init__.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/__init__.py). The [`Page`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L39) class, defined in [`browser_use/actor/page.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py), handles page-level interactions like navigation ([`goto`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L311)), JavaScript execution ([`evaluate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1846)), and screenshot capture ([`screenshot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1387)). It also integrates with Large Language Models (LLMs) for intelligent element identification ([`get_element_by_prompt`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L399)) and structured content extraction ([`extract_content`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/infrastructure/test_registry_validation.py#L70)). The [`Element`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/element.py#L62) class, found in [`browser_use/actor/element.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/element.py), provides methods for direct interaction with DOM elements, such as [`click`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L2122), [`fill`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/element.py#L353) for input fields, [`hover`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/element.py#L509), and [`screenshot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1387) of specific elements. Mouse interactions are managed by the [`Mouse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/mouse.py#L12) class in [`browser_use/actor/mouse.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/mouse.py), which dispatches CDP mouse events for precise control. Utility functions in [`browser_use/actor/utils.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/utils.py) assist with keyboard event simulation by mapping key names to browser-specific codes.

The overall browser session management is orchestrated by the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) class, which is an event-driven wrapper around a CDP client. This class, defined in [`browser_use/browser/session.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py), manages the full browser lifecycle, including starting, stopping, and resetting sessions. It leverages an internal event bus and a system of watchdogs to monitor and react to browser events. The [`SessionManager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session_manager.py#L18) in [`browser_use/browser/session_manager.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session_manager.py) further tracks and synchronizes CDP sessions and browser targets, maintaining a consistent view of the browser's state.

Efficiency is a key design consideration, leading to the implementation of lazy loading mechanisms for heavier browser components. The [`__getattr__`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/__init__.py#L110) function within [`browser_use/browser/__init__.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/__init__.py) uses to defer the loading of modules like [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) and [`BrowserProfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L538) until they are explicitly needed, which improves initial load times.

A comprehensive event system, defined in [`browser_use/browser/events.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py), facilitates communication throughout the browser automation system. This system includes various [`BaseEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/conftest.py#L34) subclasses for browser actions (e.g., [`NavigateToUrlEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L110), [`ClickElementEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L125)), browser lifecycle events ([`BrowserStartEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L292), [`BrowserStopEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L301)), DOM interactions, error handling, storage state management, and file downloads. These events are designed with Pydantic models for data validation and clear structure.

Browser profiles and configurations are handled by the [`BrowserProfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L538) class in [`browser_use/browser/profile.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py). This class allows for detailed configuration of browser launch arguments, context settings, proxy settings, and the automatic management of default extensions such as uBlock Origin.

The system also incorporates a modular "watchdog" system, with components in the [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs) directory. Each watchdog, inheriting from [`BaseWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdog_base.py#L14) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdog_base.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdog_base.py), is responsible for monitoring and managing specific aspects of browser operation, such as crash detection, DOM management, download handling, permissions, and popup management. For instance, the [`CrashWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/crash_watchdog.py#L38) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/crash_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/crash_watchdog.py) monitors browser health and detects crashes, while the [`DownloadsWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/downloads_watchdog.py#L37) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/downloads_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/downloads_watchdog.py) manages file downloads.

Integration with cloud browser services is supported through the [`CloudBrowserClient`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py#L19) in [`browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py), which provides functionalities for creating, stopping, and managing cloud browser instances, including authentication and standardized API communication models defined in [`browser_use/browser/cloud/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/views.py).

For visual feedback and debugging, a demo mode is implemented via [`browser_use/browser/demo_mode.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/demo_mode.py). This feature injects a JavaScript panel into the browser to display live log messages and events from Python. Additionally, [`browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py) provides Python-based image processing capabilities for visual debugging, allowing bounding boxes and text overlays to be drawn on screenshots to highlight specific elements.

### CDP-Use: Low-Level Browser Interaction

link

zoom\_in

The core of low-level browser interaction is provided by the [`CDP-Use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L73) library, primarily residing in the [`browser_use/actor`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor) directory. This library leverages the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) to enable direct and fine-grained control over web browsers, abstracting many of the complexities of raw CDP commands into a more manageable API. The library exposes key components for browser interaction, including [`Page`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L39), [`Element`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/element.py#L62), and [`Mouse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/mouse.py#L12) objects, which facilitate navigation, DOM manipulation, and input control.

The [`Page`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L39) class, defined in [`browser_use/actor/page.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py), represents a single browser tab or iframe and is the primary interface for page-level operations. It manages navigation tasks such as loading URLs, reloading pages, and moving through browsing history. Beyond navigation, the [`Page`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L39) class allows for the execution of arbitrary JavaScript code within the page's context and enables the capture of screenshots. It also integrates with large language models (LLMs) to perform intelligent tasks, such as identifying interactive elements on a page based on natural language prompts and extracting structured data from page content. For instance, [`get_element_by_prompt`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L399) uses an LLM to locate elements by their description, while [`extract_content`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/infrastructure/test_registry_validation.py#L70) leverages LLMs to parse page content into structured formats.

For interacting with specific components of a web page, the [`Element`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/element.py#L62) class, found in [`browser_use/actor/element.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/element.py), encapsulates individual DOM elements. This class provides methods to simulate user interactions directly on elements, including clicking, filling out input fields, hovering, focusing, and selecting options from dropdowns. It also allows for retrieving element properties like attributes and bounding box information, and even taking screenshots of individual elements. The [`Element`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/element.py#L62) class is designed to be robust, incorporating various strategies and fallbacks for interactions like clicking and filling to ensure reliability across diverse web page implementations.

The [`Mouse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/mouse.py#L12) class, specified in [`browser_use/actor/mouse.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/mouse.py), offers an interface for simulating precise mouse actions. It can perform clicks at specific coordinates, move the mouse cursor, and simulate mouse button presses and releases. A notable feature is its multi-tiered approach to scrolling, which attempts different CDP commands and falls back to JavaScript execution to ensure reliable scrolling behavior.

Utility functions for keyboard input are provided by [`Utils`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/utils.py#L4) in [`browser_use/actor/utils.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/utils.py). These utilities map human-readable key names to browser-specific key codes, which is essential for accurately simulating keyboard events.

The capabilities of this low-level browser automation are further showcased in the [`playground`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L150) directory [`browser_use/actor/playground`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/playground). Examples illustrate how to manage browser sessions, navigate through web pages, interact with elements, execute JavaScript, and perform hybrid automation tasks that combine explicit commands with AI agent interactions. For instance, [`browser_use/actor/playground/flights.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/playground/flights.py) demonstrates how direct UI interaction and AI agents can work in tandem to achieve complex tasks like finding flight information.

### Browser Session and Lifecycle Management

link

zoom\_in

The core of browser automation lies in the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) class, defined in [`browser_use/browser/session.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py). This class provides an event-driven framework for managing a web browser's lifecycle and interactions. It abstracts the complexities of the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP), allowing both high-level control for AI agents and direct interaction when needed.

The [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) handles the complete lifecycle of a browser. Upon initialization, it can configure browser launch arguments, context settings, and even manage default extensions through a [`BrowserProfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L538) object, as described in [Browser Profile and Configuration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-browser-profile-and-configuration). The [`start()`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/conftest.py#L168) method initiates the browser session, establishing a connection to the CDP endpoint, while [`stop()`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L786) and [`kill()`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L1550) methods manage graceful or forceful termination, respectively. The system supports both local browser instances and cloud-based browsers, integrating with external cloud services for creating and stopping browser instances, as detailed in [Cloud Browser Integration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-cloud-browser-integration).

A significant aspect of the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) is its event-driven architecture. It leverages an [`EventBus`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sync/service.py#L115) to facilitate communication between different components. Browser actions, such as navigation, clicks, or text input, dispatch events, and specialized components called "watchdogs" listen for and react to these events. This modular design, with a variety of watchdogs responsible for specific functionalities, is discussed in [Watchdog System for Browser Operations](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-watchdog-system-for-browser-operations). Examples include watchdogs for managing downloads, handling storage states, or enforcing security policies.

Tab management is also central to the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94). It tracks the currently focused tab using an [`agent_focus_target_id`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L413) and provides mechanisms for navigating to URLs, creating new tabs, switching between existing tabs, and closing tabs. Upon tab changes or other significant browser state modifications, the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) ensures that cached DOM information and browser state summaries are cleared and updated. This ensures that the system always operates with the most current view of the browser, which is particularly important for AI agents.

To ensure a consistent view of the browser's state, the [`SessionManager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session_manager.py#L18) in [`browser_use/browser/session_manager.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session_manager.py) dynamically tracks CDP sessions and targets. It listens for CDP events like [`Target.attachedToTarget`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L508) and [`Target.detachedFromTarget`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session_manager.py#L3) to keep its internal state synchronized. This manager is also responsible for automatically recovering agent focus if the currently active tab or target unexpectedly detaches, ensuring that automation tasks can continue seamlessly.

Furthermore, the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) integrates features for visual feedback and debugging, such as a demo mode that injects an in-browser log panel for live event display, as discussed in [Demo Mode and Visual Logging](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-demo-mode-and-visual-logging), and Python-based image processing to highlight elements on screenshots for visual debugging, described in [Visual Debugging and Element Highlighting](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-visual-debugging-and-element-highlighting).

The browser automation system employs a comprehensive event-driven communication system, centered around the [`BaseEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/conftest.py#L34) class, to manage interactions within and across different components. This approach enables various parts of the system, such as agents and internal browser modules, to communicate without direct dependencies, promoting modularity and scalability. All events are defined as [`pydantic`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L15) models, ensuring type safety and predictable data structures throughout the system.

Events are categorized into several groups to reflect different aspects of browser operation. High-level browser actions, initiated by agents or tools, include commands to [`NavigateToUrlEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L110), [`ClickElementEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L125), [`TypeTextEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L147), and [`ScrollEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L159). These events encapsulate the desired browser interaction along with any necessary parameters, such as a target URL, DOM node, or text input. The system also manages browser lifecycle events, such as [`BrowserStartEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L292) and [`BrowserStopEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L301), which signify the beginning and end of a browser session, or [`BrowserLaunchEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L316) and [`BrowserKillEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L324) for managing the browser process itself.

DOM-related events convey changes or states within the browser's Document Object Model and browsing context. Examples include [`TabCreatedEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L391) and [`TabClosedEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L400) for tab management, and [`NavigationStartedEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L438) and [`NavigationCompleteEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L447) to track the navigation process. Error conditions are communicated via [`BrowserErrorEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L464), providing details about the nature of the issue.

For persistent browser sessions, events like [`SaveStorageStateEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L479) and [`LoadStorageStateEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L497) handle the management of browser storage, including cookies and local storage. File downloads initiated within the browser are tracked through [`DownloadStartedEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L524), [`DownloadProgressEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L535), and [`FileDownloadedEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L546), providing real-time updates on the download status.

The system also includes specific events for unique scenarios, such as [`AboutBlankDVDScreensaverShownEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L562), which indicates a visual animation on [`about:blank`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L1308) tabs, and [`DialogOpenedEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L569) for JavaScript dialogs. The use of [`field_validator`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L10) within events like [`ClickElementEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L125) ensures that complex objects, such as [`EnhancedDOMTreeNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L373), are properly serialized, specifically by nullifying circular references to prevent data corruption and ensure efficient event handling. Additionally, a runtime check ensures event names are unique and consistently suffixed with "Event," preventing naming ambiguities. This entire event system is primarily defined within the [`browser_use/browser/events.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py) file.

### Browser Profile and Configuration

Browser profiles are managed to provide a configurable and consistent browser environment for automation tasks. The core [`BrowserProfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L538) class, defined in [`browser_use/browser/profile.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py), aggregates various settings necessary for launching and configuring a browser instance. These settings include command-line arguments passed to the browser executable, context settings that define the browsing environment (such as user agent or viewport size), and proxy settings for routing network traffic.

The configuration also encompasses the automatic management of default browser extensions. For example, [`BrowserProfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L538) ensures that extensions like uBlock Origin are downloaded, extracted, and integrated into the browser profile. This process can involve patching extensions to pre-populate settings, such as whitelisting domains for ad blockers, to optimize browser behavior for automation. Utility functions in [`browser_use/browser/profile.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py) handle the download and extraction of [`.crx`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L688) files and the application of patches to extension configurations.

When a browser session starts, the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) class (described in [Browser Session and Lifecycle Management](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-browser-session-and-lifecycle-management)), utilizes the [`BrowserProfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L538) to apply these settings. This includes compiling a comprehensive set of Chrome CLI arguments, which can override or merge with default arguments to fine-tune browser behavior for specific tasks, including security, performance, and headless operation. The system also dynamically detects display configurations to adapt browser window and viewport settings, ensuring a consistent visual experience across different environments. Pydantic models are used extensively within [`browser_use/browser/profile.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py) to provide structured, type-hinted, and validated configurations, enhancing the robustness and maintainability of browser profile management.

The lazy loading mechanism, explained in [Browser Automation Core](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core), defers the instantiation of [`BrowserProfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L538) and related heavy components until they are explicitly needed, improving initial application load times. This ensures that the overhead of profile management is only incurred when necessary.

### Watchdog System for Browser Operations

link

Watchdog Name

File Path

Description

`AboutBlankWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/aboutblank_watchdog.py`

Manages `about:blank` tabs, ensuring one is always present with a DVD screensaver animation, especially when other tabs are closed.

`CrashWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/crash_watchdog.py`

Monitors browser health for crashes, network timeouts, and unresponsive sessions using CDP. It also handles redirection of `chrome://new-tab-page/` to `about:blank`.

`DefaultActionWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/default_action_watchdog.py`

Provides core browser interaction functionalities such as clicking, typing, scrolling, navigating history, refreshing pages, sending keys, and uploading files, with built-in download detection and print handling.

`DOMWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/dom_watchdog.py`

Manages the browser's DOM tree, handles DOM snapshotting, element selection, and provides methods for building and serializing the DOM state. It also integrates with other watchdogs to deliver a comprehensive `BrowserStateSummary`.

`DownloadsWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/downloads_watchdog.py`

Monitors and handles file downloads, including auto-downloading PDFs and detecting downloads triggered by network responses. Supports direct callbacks for immediate synchronization with other components.

`HarRecordingWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/har_recording_watchdog.py`

Records all HTTPS network activity throughout a browser session and outputs it into a HAR 1.2 format file upon browser shutdown.

`LocalBrowserWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/local_browser_watchdog.py`

Manages the lifecycle of a local browser subprocess, including launching, finding free ports, and ensuring proper cleanup on termination.

`PermissionsWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/permissions_watchdog.py`

Automatically grants specified browser permissions upon connection, ensuring the browser operates with necessary access rights.

`PopupsWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/popups_watchdog.py`

Automatically handles JavaScript dialogs (like `alert`, `confirm`, `prompt`) by accepting or dismissing them immediately to prevent automation from stalling.

`RecordingWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/recording_watchdog.py`

Manages video recording of the browser session using CDP screencasting, capturing frames and compiling them into a video file. It can switch recording focus between active tabs.

`ScreenshotWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/screenshot_watchdog.py`

Manages and executes screenshot requests using CDP, providing functionality to capture full-page or clipped screenshots.

`SecurityWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/security_watchdog.py`

Enforces URL access policies, blocking navigation to or tab creation with disallowed domains and optionally blocking direct IP address access.

`StorageStateWatchdog`

`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/storage_state_watchdog.py`

Manages the persistence of browser storage state, including cookies and `localStorage`, saving and loading them from a specified file path. It also auto-saves changes periodically or on event.

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) framework employs a modular "watchdog" system to continuously monitor and manage various aspects of a browser's operation. This system consists of specialized components, each derived from the [`BaseWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdog_base.py#L14) class defined in [`browser_use/browser/watchdog_base.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdog_base.py), that observe the browser's state and react to changes by emitting events. This architecture allows for robust and decoupled management of browser behaviors, including crash detection, DOM management, download handling, permissions, popup management, and security policies.

One key component is the [`AboutBlankWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/aboutblank_watchdog.py#L24) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/aboutblank_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/aboutblank_watchdog.py), which ensures the browser remains operational by always keeping at least one [`about:blank`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L1308) tab open. This prevents the browser from closing if all other functional tabs are closed. It also injects a visual screensaver into these tabs to indicate activity.

The [`CrashWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/crash_watchdog.py#L38) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/crash_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/crash_watchdog.py) monitors browser health, detecting issues such as crashes, network timeouts, and unresponsiveness. Upon detection of such events, it dispatches [`BrowserErrorEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L464) to signal problems.

Browser interactions, such as clicks, typing, scrolling, and file uploads, are handled by the [`DefaultActionWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/default_action_watchdog.py#L41) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/default_action_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/default_action_watchdog.py). This watchdog uses the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) for precise control and includes logic for robust element interaction, such as occlusion checks and compatibility with various JavaScript frameworks. It can also handle special cases like detecting print buttons and generating PDFs directly.

For managing the browser's Document Object Model (DOM), the [`DOMWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/dom_watchdog.py#L26) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/dom_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/dom_watchdog.py) is responsible. It builds and serializes the DOM tree, aggregates browser state information (including screenshots and network requests), and provides utilities for accessing and analyzing DOM elements.

File downloads are managed by the [`DownloadsWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/downloads_watchdog.py#L37) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/downloads_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/downloads_watchdog.py). It monitors both browser-level and network-level events to detect and track downloads, including automatic PDF downloads. It dispatches events related to download lifecycle and provides callbacks for synchronous handling.

The [`HarRecordingWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/har_recording_watchdog.py#L144) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/har_recording_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/har_recording_watchdog.py) records all HTTPS network activity during a browser session and saves it as a HAR 1.2 file upon shutdown. This is useful for debugging and analyzing network interactions.

For local browser instances, the [`LocalBrowserWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/local_browser_watchdog.py#L27) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/local_browser_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/local_browser_watchdog.py) manages the browser subprocess lifecycle, including launching, monitoring, and cleaning up. It can find browser executables, handle launch errors, and ensure proper process termination and resource cleanup.

The [`PermissionsWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/permissions_watchdog.py#L14) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/permissions_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/permissions_watchdog.py) grants specified browser permissions when a browser connection is established, using CDP [`Browser.grantPermissions`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L2915). This ensures the browser operates with necessary permissions without manual intervention.

To handle browser popups, the [`PopupsWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/popups_watchdog.py#L13) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/popups_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/popups_watchdog.py) automatically accepts or dismisses JavaScript dialogs. The [`RecordingWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/recording_watchdog.py#L19) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/recording_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/recording_watchdog.py) manages video recording of browser sessions using CDP screencasting.

The [`ScreenshotWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/screenshot_watchdog.py#L17) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/screenshot_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/screenshot_watchdog.py) processes [`ScreenshotEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L185)s, capturing base64-encoded PNG screenshots of page/tab targets. Finally, the [`SecurityWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/security_watchdog.py#L22) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/security_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/security_watchdog.py) enforces URL access policies, blocking disallowed URLs or redirecting to safe pages based on configured allowed and prohibited domains. The [`StorageStateWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/storage_state_watchdog.py#L25) in [`browser_use/browser/watchdogs/storage_state_watchdog.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/storage_state_watchdog.py) manages browser storage state (cookies, local storage, session storage), saving and loading it as needed.

This collection of watchdogs ensures comprehensive management and monitoring of browser operations, facilitating robust and reliable automation within the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) framework.

zoom\_in

Integration with cloud browser services is managed through the [`CloudBrowserClient`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py#L19), which handles the creation, termination, and general management of cloud-based browser instances. This client utilizes an asynchronous HTTP client to communicate with the cloud service API. Key functionalities include [`create_browser`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py#L27) for provisioning a new browser instance and [`stop_browser`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py#L104) for releasing resources. These operations are governed by request and response models, such as [`CreateBrowserRequest`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/views.py#L28) and [`CloudBrowserResponse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/views.py#L67), defined in [`browser_use/browser/cloud/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/views.py). These models ensure structured data exchange and validation with the cloud API.

Authentication to the cloud browser service is managed by retrieving an API token, primarily from the [`BROWSER_USE_API_KEY`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L19) environment variable or a configuration file. The [`CloudBrowserClient`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py#L19) in [`browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py) is designed to handle various HTTP status codes and network errors, raising specific exceptions like [`CloudBrowserAuthError`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/views.py#L86) for authentication failures or [`CloudBrowserError`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/views.py#L80) for general service issues. This robust error handling helps in diagnosing and managing interactions with the cloud service. The system also tracks the [`current_session_id`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py#L25) to manage the active cloud browser session, simplifying commands like [`stop_browser`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/cloud/cloud.py#L104) when a specific session ID is not provided. Within a [Browser Session and Lifecycle Management](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-browser-session-and-lifecycle-management), the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) class can be configured to use a cloud browser by setting relevant parameters in its [`BrowserProfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L538).

### Demo Mode and Visual Logging

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?>
<!DOCTYPE svg PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD SVG 1.1//EN"
 "http://www.w3.org/Graphics/SVG/1.1/DTD/svg11.dtd">
<!-- Generated by graphviz version 2.44.1 (20201121.0304)
 -->

The system includes a demo mode for visual logging that injects a JavaScript panel into the browser to display live log messages and events dispatched from Python. This allows for real-time visual feedback and debugging directly within the browser interface. The [`DemoMode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/demo_mode.py#L800) class in [`browser_use/browser/demo_mode.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/demo_mode.py) manages the injection of this JavaScript panel and the broadcasting of log messages.

The JavaScript within the panel is responsible for rendering and styling the log panel, managing its state (open/closed, messages, expanded entries) using [`sessionStorage`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L1202), and processing incoming log entries. Log messages are formatted and appended to the panel, allowing for interactive expansion and collapse of longer messages.

The Python [`DemoMode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/demo_mode.py#L800) class controls the browser-side panel through the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) object. It ensures the panel's readiness by injecting the JavaScript into new and existing browser pages using the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). Log messages are dispatched from Python via [`send_log`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/demo_mode.py#L845) as custom JavaScript events, which the injected script then captures and displays. This mechanism facilitates visual logging that aids in debugging and demonstrating browser automation processes.

For additional information on how browser sessions are managed, see [Browser Session and Lifecycle Management](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-browser-session-and-lifecycle-management). The broader event system that facilitates such communication is described in [Event System for Browser Communication](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-event-system-for-browser-communication).
