The system provides functionality for visual debugging by drawing bounding boxes and text overlays directly on screenshots of web pages. This Python-based image processing approach, primarily implemented in [`browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py), replaces JavaScript-based highlighting. This method is particularly useful for visually identifying interactive DOM elements, assisting in debugging, and guiding automation processes.

The core of this functionality lies within the [`create_highlighted_screenshot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py#L409) asynchronous function. This function takes a base64-encoded screenshot and a map of interactive DOM elements, then decodes the screenshot and uses an [`ImageDraw.Draw`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/gif.py#L226) object for rendering. It iterates through the interactive elements, calling [`process_element_highlight`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py#L340) for each to draw visual cues. Accurate scaling is ensured by using the [`device_pixel_ratio`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L201) to translate CSS pixel coordinates to device pixel coordinates, critical for precise highlighting across varying screen resolutions. The output is a base64-encoded PNG image. The [`create_highlighted_screenshot_async`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py#L502) function wraps this process, optionally retrieving viewport information from a [`cdp_session`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docs/customize/hooks.mdx#L49) using [`get_viewport_info_from_cdp`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py#L470) to ensure accurate positioning even with page scrolling.

Individual element highlighting is managed by [`process_element_highlight`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py#L340), which scales element coordinates and determines a highlight color based on the element's tag name and type using [`get_element_color`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py#L92). An index is generated for each element, and [`draw_enhanced_bounding_box_with_text`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py#L108) renders a dashed bounding box along with a textual overlay. To optimize performance, [`_FONT_CACHE`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py#L22) and [`get_cross_platform_font`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/python_highlights.py#L36) implement font caching, preventing repetitive font loading. The system prioritizes visual clarity by adjusting the position of index overlays for small elements to avoid obscuring content.

This Python-based approach ensures consistent visual feedback, independent of the browser's JavaScript execution, which is beneficial for debugging automated browser interactions or guiding AI agents by visually marking interactive components. The [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) also incorporates a [`ScreenshotWatchdog`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/watchdogs/screenshot_watchdog.py#L17) as part of its [Watchdog System for Browser Operations](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-watchdog-system-for-browser-operations), which processes [`ScreenshotEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L185)s. For more details on browser session management, refer to [Browser Session and Lifecycle Management](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-browser-session-and-lifecycle-management).

link

zoom\_in

The system integrates and orchestrates AI agents to perform advanced browser automation tasks. This involves managing the agent's state, integrating with Large Language Models (LLMs) for decision-making and action generation, executing browser actions, maintaining conversation history, and interacting with a virtual file system.

The core of this system is the [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) class, defined in [`browser_use/agent/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py), which acts as an orchestrator. This agent is designed to execute tasks in a web browser autonomously. It leverages LLMs to interpret current browser states, decide on subsequent actions, and track progress toward a given objective. The agent's operational loop involves preparing the context (such as DOM information and screenshots), interacting with an LLM to generate actions, executing these actions within the browser, and then post-processing to update its internal state, record history, and log outcomes. For instance, it can detect and mitigate repetitive behaviors, encouraging more effective planning through mechanisms like [`planning_replan_on_stall`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L78) and [`planning_exploration_limit`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L79) settings.

Central to the agent's functionality is its communication with LLMs. The system utilizes structured output through Pydantic models for [`AgentOutput`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L393) and [`AgentStructuredOutput`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L613), ensuring that LLM responses are reliable and easily parsable. A [`MessageManager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py#L99), located in [`browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py), is responsible for building and compacting the message history for LLM prompts. This includes handling sensitive data by replacing it with placeholders before being sent to the LLM, as outlined in [`browser_use/agent/message_manager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager). The [`SystemPrompt`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py#L27) and [`AgentMessagePrompt`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py#L103) classes in [`browser_use/agent/prompts.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py) are crucial for crafting the initial system message and detailed user messages that guide the LLM's behavior, sometimes including visual context from screenshots for multimodal LLMs. The system includes various prompt templates in [`browser_use/agent/system_prompts`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/system_prompts) that define the agent's purpose, capabilities, and operational rules, ensuring consistent interaction with web environments.

Observability and evaluation are facilitated through tools such as the [`create_history_gif`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/gif.py#L35) function in [`browser_use/agent/gif.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/gif.py), which generates visual logs of agent execution by overlaying task descriptions onto screenshots. Additionally, the [`construct_judge_messages`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/judge.py#L44) and [`construct_simple_judge_messages`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/judge.py#L226) functions in [`browser_use/agent/judge.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/judge.py) construct detailed or simple prompts for LLMs to evaluate the agent's execution traces, including multimodal support via screenshots. Cloud-related events, such as task updates and session management, are structured via Pydantic models defined in [`browser_use/agent/cloud_events.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/cloud_events.py) for external monitoring and reporting.

The system supports a wide array of LLMs from different providers, providing a unified asynchronous invocation interface through the [`BaseChatModel`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/base.py#L18) protocol, defined in [`browser_use/llm/base.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/base.py). This standardization ensures consistent interaction with LLMs regardless of their underlying provider, which is managed in the [`browser_use/llm`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm) directory. This directory also provides components for standardized message structures, robust error handling, and lazy loading mechanisms for LLM instances, as well as a [`SchemaOptimizer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/schema.py#L10) in [`browser_use/llm/schema.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/schema.py) for structured output. Specific integrations for providers like Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Google Gemini, Groq, Mistral, OCI, Ollama, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Cerebras, DeepSeek, and Vercel AI Gateway are handled in their respective subdirectories within [`browser_use/llm`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm).

zoom\_in

The [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) class in [`browser_use/agent/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py) is central to orchestrating autonomous browser automation tasks. It manages the lifecycle of an agent from task initiation to completion, integrating large language models (LLMs) for decision-making and executing actions within a browser environment.

The agent's primary responsibility is task execution, which involves a continuous loop of preparing context, interacting with an LLM to determine the next actions, and then executing those actions. The [`run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L1073) method in [`browser_use/agent/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py) orchestrates the overall task by repeatedly calling [`step`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L1016), which is responsible for a single logical action within the agent's workflow.

State preparation is a critical aspect of this process. Before each LLM interaction, the agent gathers comprehensive information about the current browser state, including DOM details, screenshots, and the current URL. It also updates available file paths and incorporates any messages from the [Agent Message and Prompt Management](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#ai-agent-integration-and-orchestration-agent-message-and-prompt-management) system. This prepared context is then sent to the LLM to inform its decision-making.

Action execution involves processing the actions specified by the LLM and performing them within the browser. The agent handles various types of browser interactions, file system operations, and external tool calls through a [`Tools`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L345) instance. Error handling is built into the agent's core, with [`_handle_step_error`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L1212) in [`browser_use/agent/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py) managing consecutive failures and logging any exceptions that occur during a step. This includes mechanisms for retrying LLM calls or falling back to alternative LLMs in case of rate limits or provider errors, as implemented in [`_get_model_output_with_retry`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L1589).

For observability and debugging, the agent supports visual logging and LLM-based trace evaluation. The [`create_history_gif`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/gif.py#L35) function in [`browser_use/agent/gif.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/gif.py) generates animated GIFs from the agent's execution history, overlaying task descriptions and current goals onto screenshots. This provides a visual record of the agent's actions and progress. For more detailed evaluation, functions like [`construct_judge_messages`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/judge.py#L44) in [`browser_use/agent/judge.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/judge.py) create structured prompts for LLMs to act as judges, evaluating the agent's entire trace against a given task and, optionally, ground truth. This process can incorporate screenshots for multimodal evaluation.

Action loop detection is managed by the [`ActionLoopDetector`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L155) in [`browser_use/agent/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py). This component tracks recent actions and page fingerprints to identify repetitive behavior or page stagnation. When a potential loop is detected, it generates "nudge" messages for the LLM, encouraging it to break out of unproductive cycles and adapt its strategy. The [`_normalize_action_for_hash`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L108) and [`compute_action_hash`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L149) utilities in [`browser_use/agent/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py) contribute to this by creating stable identifiers for actions, enabling consistent loop detection.

The agent's state is meticulously managed using Pydantic models defined in [`browser_use/agent/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py). Key models include [`AgentState`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L249), which tracks runtime metrics like step count and failures; [`AgentOutput`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L393), which defines the structured format for LLM responses; and [`AgentHistoryList`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L616), which stores a complete chronological record of the agent's execution, including model outputs, action results, and browser states. These models facilitate data validation, serialization, and enable comprehensive analysis of agent performance. Furthermore, cloud events are dispatched using models defined in [`browser_use/agent/cloud_events.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/cloud_events.py) to provide real-time updates and logs for external monitoring systems.

### Agent Message and Prompt Management

zoom\_in

The agent's message and prompt management system is responsible for shaping the conversational context provided to Large Language Models (LLMs), enabling them to make informed decisions and generate effective actions. This system primarily revolves around the [`MessageManager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py#L99) and prompt generation classes like [`SystemPrompt`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py#L27) and [`AgentMessagePrompt`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py#L103).

Central to this is the [`MessageManager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py#L99), defined in [`browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py), which maintains a detailed [`agent_history_items`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/views.py#L91) to track the agent's step-by-step execution. This history is crucial for the LLM to understand the progression of a task. To prevent LLM context windows from being overwhelmed, the [`MessageManager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py#L99) implements a message compaction strategy. This involves summarizing older [`agent_history_items`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/views.py#L91) into a [`compacted_memory`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/views.py#L97) block, using an LLM to generate the summary when a certain [`trigger_char_count`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L39) or [`step_number`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/views.py#L18) threshold is reached. This ensures that the LLM always receives a concise yet comprehensive overview of past interactions.

Another key aspect of [`MessageManager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py#L99) is its sensitive data filtering capabilities. It identifies and replaces sensitive values, such as API keys or personal information, within messages with generic [`<secret>`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L541) placeholders before they are sent to the LLM. This safeguards private data while still conveying the structural information the LLM needs. The [`MessageManager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/service.py#L99) also prepares the complete [`UserMessage`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/messages.py#L135) for the LLM, integrating various contextual elements like the current browser state summary, file system contents, and any relevant screenshots or images from file reads.

The [`SystemPrompt`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py#L27) class, instantiated in [`browser_use/agent/prompts.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py), is responsible for crafting the initial, guiding message for the LLM. It loads pre-defined Markdown templates from the [`browser_use/agent/system_prompts`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/system_prompts) directory, which articulate the agent's core purpose, operational rules, input/output formats, and planning methodologies. These templates are crucial for setting the LLM's "mental model" and ensuring consistent behavior, whether it's for general operation, "flash mode," or scenarios where explicit "thinking" blocks are enabled or disabled.

Complementing the [`SystemPrompt`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py#L27), the [`AgentMessagePrompt`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py#L103) class, also in [`browser_use/agent/prompts.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/prompts.py), constructs the detailed [`UserMessage`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/messages.py#L135) at each step. This message aggregates dynamic information such as the browser's current state (including DOM statistics and recent events), the agent's current state (user request, file system description, plan), and visual context through screenshots. For multi-modal LLMs, it intelligently includes resized screenshots and images read from files, providing visual ground truth to the agent. It also handles edge cases like conditionally disabling vision for initial page loads to optimize token usage.

Together, these components ensure that the LLM receives a carefully curated and appropriately structured input at every interaction, enabling it to effectively reason, plan, and execute browser automation tasks while respecting privacy and computational constraints. The Pydantic models defined in [`browser_use/agent/message_manager/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/views.py), such as [`HistoryItem`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/views.py#L15), [`MessageHistory`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/views.py#L66), and [`MessageManagerState`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/message_manager/views.py#L86), underpin this system by providing robust, validated data structures for managing the agent's conversational state.

### Unified LLM Interface and Core Components

zoom\_in

The framework for integrating various Large Language Models (LLMs) into the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) ecosystem is designed to provide a consistent and abstract interface for interacting with different providers. This standardization is achieved through the [`BaseChatModel`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/base.py#L18) protocol, which defines a uniform asynchronous invocation method for all LLMs, regardless of their specific API or implementation. This approach ensures that developers can switch between different LLM providers with minimal code changes.

Core to this unified interface are the standardized message and content structures, which mirror common LLM API formats (e.g., OpenAI's message types). These structures, defined in files such as [`browser_use/llm/messages.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/messages.py), facilitate the representation of chat messages ([`UserMessage`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/messages.py#L135), [`SystemMessage`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/messages.py#L168), [`AssistantMessage`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/messages.py#L196)), various content parts (text, image, refusal statements), and function or tool calls. This consistency simplifies prompt creation and response parsing across diverse LLMs, crucial for browser automation tasks where varied input and structured output are common.

To optimize application startup performance and resource utilization, the system employs lazy loading mechanisms for both LLM classes and pre-configured instances. This means that LLMs are only instantiated and their dependencies loaded when they are explicitly accessed, rather than at application launch. This dynamic loading is managed by the central [`browser_use/llm/__init__.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/__init__.py) file.

A key capability for interacting with LLMs is the [`SchemaOptimizer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/schema.py#L10), detailed in [`browser_use/llm/schema.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/schema.py). This utility is responsible for generating optimized JSON schemas from Pydantic models. It addresses challenges like resolving references and ensuring compatibility with the strict JSON parsing requirements of certain LLMs by explicitly setting [`additionalProperties: false`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L15) and defining required fields. This allows [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) to leverage structured outputs from LLMs for precise data extraction and action generation. The [`ainvoke`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/base.py#L35) method, part of the [`BaseChatModel`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/base.py#L18) protocol, can directly accept a Pydantic model as an [`output_format`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/conftest.py#L131) parameter, enabling type-safe parsing of LLM responses into structured data.

Error handling is also standardized, with custom exception classes such as [`ModelError`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/exceptions.py#L1), [`ModelProviderError`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/exceptions.py#L5), and [`ModelRateLimitError`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/exceptions.py#L20) (defined in [`browser_use/llm/exceptions.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/exceptions.py)). This hierarchy provides clear and consistent error reporting across all integrated LLM providers, simplifying debugging and error management. For details on how specific LLMs are integrated, refer to [Provider-Specific LLM Integrations](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#ai-agent-integration-and-orchestration-unified-llm-interface-and-core-components-provider-specific-llm-integrations).

#### Provider-Specific LLM Integrations

OpenAI-compatible API for Vercel AI Gateway, configurable provider options, custom schema fixing for Google models.

The repository includes integrations for various Large Language Model (LLM) providers, each designed to provide a consistent interface despite underlying API differences. These integrations primarily focus on managing API communication, serializing messages into provider-specific formats, handling structured output (often using Pydantic models), and providing robust error handling.

For instance, the Anthropic integration, found in the [`browser_use/llm/anthropic`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/anthropic) directory, uses a [`ChatAnthropic`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/anthropic/chat.py#L33) class to manage API calls and model configurations. It features an [`AnthropicMessageSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/anthropic/serializer.py#L27) that adapts generic message objects into the [`MessageParam`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/anthropic/serializer.py#L8) format required by the Anthropic API, handling content types like text, images, and tool calls. Structured output is achieved by generating [`ToolParam`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/anthropic/chat.py#L15) definitions from Pydantic schemas, and the integration includes error handling for common Anthropic API exceptions.

Similarly, the AWS Bedrock integration, located in the [`browser_use/llm/aws`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/aws) directory, offers [`ChatAWSBedrock`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/aws/chat_bedrock.py#L22) for general Bedrock models and [`ChatAnthropicBedrock`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/aws/chat_anthropic.py#L32) for Anthropic Claude models hosted on Bedrock. These classes handle AWS authentication, model configuration, and structured output using Bedrock's tool-calling capabilities. The [`AWSBedrockMessageSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/aws/serializer.py#L18) in [`browser_use/llm/aws/serializer.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/aws/serializer.py) converts internal message representations into Bedrock's JSON format, supporting multimodal content including base64-encoded images. A lazy loading mechanism in [`browser_use/llm/aws/__init__.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/aws/__init__.py) optimizes startup time by deferring imports until needed.

The Azure OpenAI integration, defined in [`browser_use/llm/azure/chat.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/azure/chat.py), uses the [`ChatAzureOpenAI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/azure/chat.py#L34) class to interact with Azure OpenAI models. This integration dynamically routes requests to either the standard Chat Completions API or the Azure-specific Responses API, depending on the model. It supports structured output via Pydantic models and handles Azure-specific API configurations and error types.

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) cloud API client, provided by the [`ChatBrowserUse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/browser_use/chat.py#L31) class in [`browser_use/llm/browser_use/chat.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/browser_use/chat.py), facilitates communication with the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) cloud API for optimized browser automation LLM inference. This client manages API key authentication, model selection, request serialization, and incorporates robust retry mechanisms with exponential backoff for transient errors. It also supports structured output through Pydantic models.

For Cerebras LLMs, the [`ChatCerebras`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/cerebras/chat.py#L28) class in [`browser_use/llm/cerebras/chat.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/cerebras/chat.py) acts as an OpenAI-compatible wrapper. It supports both standard chat completions and structured JSON output by dynamically injecting Pydantic JSON schemas into the prompt, guiding the LLM to produce the desired format. The [`CerebrasMessageSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/cerebras/serializer.py#L19) in [`browser_use/llm/cerebras/serializer.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/cerebras/serializer.py) handles the conversion of messages into a format compatible with Cerebras models.

Each integration ensures that despite varying API structures and features, the core application can interact with different LLM providers through a unified and consistent interface. This abstraction simplifies the development of AI agents by centralizing message handling, output parsing, and error management logic. For a broader understanding of the unified LLM interface, refer to [Unified LLM Interface and Core Components](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#ai-agent-integration-and-orchestration-unified-llm-interface-and-core-components).

This section focuses on the mechanisms for executing Python code within the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) environment, particularly for browser automation tasks, both locally and remotely. It provides an overview of the notebook-like Python code execution environment, which offers Large Language Models (LLMs) persistent namespace access and control over the browser. It also covers type-safe sandboxing for remote function execution, including serialization and event streaming.

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) environment provides a "Code-Use Mode" where an LLM can generate and execute Python code to automate browser interactions. This system is designed to mimic a Jupyter notebook, allowing for an iterative and programmatic approach to web automation. The [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) in [`browser_use/code_use/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py) orchestrates this process, managing the interaction between an LLM, a browser session, and a persistent Python execution environment. This setup allows the LLM to write incremental Python code that operates within a persistent namespace, enabling it to carry variables and functions across execution steps.

A core aspect of this mode is the [`create_namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L256) function in [`browser_use/code_use/namespace.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py). This function initializes a rich Python execution environment with direct access to browser control functions like [`navigate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L411), [`click`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L2122), [`input_text`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/infrastructure/test_registry_validation.py#L210), and [`evaluate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1846). These functions allow the LLM to control the browser, interact with elements, and execute JavaScript code directly within the browser context. The [`evaluate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1846) function, in particular, is significant as it allows for direct Document Object Model (DOM) manipulation and data extraction, bridging the gap between Python logic and browser-side operations. The [`system_prompt.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L170) in [`browser_use/code_use/system_prompt.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/system_prompt.md) guides the LLM's "Mental Model" and "STRATEGY" for generating Python code, interacting with the browser, and iterative debugging, emphasizing an index-based interaction strategy.

For remote execution and enhanced security, [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) incorporates a type-safe sandboxing mechanism. This is primarily handled by the [`sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L215) decorator in [`browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py). This decorator transforms an asynchronous Python function into one that executes remotely within an isolated environment. The decorator manages the complexities of serialization and deserialization, using [`cloudpickle`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L47) and [`base64`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L355) encoding to package function code, parameters, and closure variables for transfer to the remote sandbox. This ensures that complex Python objects are correctly reconstructed in the remote environment.

Communication with the sandboxed environment relies on Server-Sent Events (SSE), enabling real-time feedback such as browser creation events, logs, and execution results. The [`SSEEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L64) models defined in [`browser_use/sandbox/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py) structure these events, facilitating incremental updates and final results back to the client. Upon receiving results from the sandbox, the [`_parse_with_type_annotation`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L533) function in [`browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py) reconstructs complex Python objects based on the decorated function's return type annotation, ensuring that the remote function's return value matches the expected local Python type. This mechanism provides a robust and type-safe way to execute browser automation tasks in a distributed or secure manner.

### Notebook-Like Code Execution for LLMs

The [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) system facilitates browser automation by enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate and execute Python code in a persistent, Jupyter-like environment. This approach allows LLMs to control browser actions, manage data, and perform iterative tasks with a rich set of browser control functions and data science libraries. The system captures the entire execution history and state for evaluation and debugging, promoting a dynamic and adaptive workflow.

The core of this system is the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) class, which orchestrates the interaction between the LLM, a browser session, and the Python execution environment. It iteratively requests Python code from the LLM, executes it, and provides the LLM with updated browser state. The execution environment, created by [`create_namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L256) in [`browser_use/code_use/namespace.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py), is populated with browser control functions such as [`navigate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L411), [`click`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L2122), [`input_text`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/infrastructure/test_registry_validation.py#L210), [`scroll`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1241), and [`evaluate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1846). These functions allow the LLM to interact with web pages, perform navigation, and execute JavaScript directly within the browser context. The [`evaluate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1846) function, in particular, is designed to enable direct DOM manipulation and structured data extraction through JavaScript, bridging the gap between Python logic and browser-side operations. The namespace also includes standard Python libraries ([`json`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L30), [`asyncio`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L107), [`Path`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L316), [`csv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L1620), [`re`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L980), [`datetime`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L6), [`requests`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L7)) and can optionally integrate data science libraries like [`numpy`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L64), [`pandas`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L64), and [`BeautifulSoup`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/README.md?plain=1#L42) if they are installed, enhancing the agent's data processing capabilities.

The agent's state and execution flow are meticulously managed. The [`format_browser_state_for_llm`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/formatting.py#L12) function in [`browser_use/code_use/formatting.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/formatting.py) structures the current browser and namespace information into a human-readable format for the LLM. This includes the current URL, page title, tab information, scroll position, pending network requests, and available variables, along with a truncated representation of the Document Object Model (DOM). This formatted output provides the LLM with the necessary context to make informed decisions for its next code generation step. The LLM's behavior is guided by a [`system_prompt.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L170) in [`browser_use/code_use/system_prompt.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/system_prompt.md), which outlines a mental model and strategy for generating Python code, interacting with the browser, handling data, and performing iterative debugging.

To support analysis and reproducibility, the system provides mechanisms for managing and exporting code execution sessions. The [`views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/README.md?plain=1#L28) file in [`browser_use/code_use/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py) defines data models like [`CodeCell`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L32), [`ExecutionStatus`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L23), and [`NotebookSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L47) to track the state and history of code execution. [`CodeAgentHistory`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L165) and [`CodeAgentHistoryList`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L187) store detailed logs, including LLM outputs, execution results, browser states, and metadata. Sessions can be exported to Jupyter notebooks ([`.ipynb`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py#L14)) or standalone Python scripts using [`export_to_ipynb`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py#L12) and [`session_to_python_script`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py#L177) functions in [`browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py). These export utilities ensure that the generated notebooks or scripts are directly executable, preserving code, outputs, browser state, and embedded JavaScript logic, which is critical for review and further development.

#### Persistent Namespace and Browser Control Functions

zoom\_in

The [`create_namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L256) function, defined in [`browser_use/code_use/namespace.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py), establishes a Python execution environment tailored for Large Language Models (LLMs) to interact with a web browser. This namespace is persistent, resembling a Jupyter notebook's state, and provides LLMs with direct programmatic control over browser actions and access to common data science utilities.

The core of this environment is the [`browser`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/browser/save_cookies.py#L14) object, which grants LLMs the ability to execute fundamental browser operations such as [`navigate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L411) to a URL, [`click`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L2122) on elements, [`input_text`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/infrastructure/test_registry_validation.py#L210) into forms, and [`evaluate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1846) JavaScript directly within the browser's context. A crucial component of this functionality is the [`evaluate_wrapper`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L333), which enhances the raw [`evaluate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1846) function. This wrapper intelligently handles injecting Python variables into JavaScript code and automatically encloses the JavaScript in Immediately Invoked Function Expressions (IIFEs) if needed. This simplifies the LLM's task of generating JavaScript by abstracting away common boilerplate, making the process more robust and less error-prone. The [`get_selector_from_index_wrapper`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L447) provides a method to derive CSS selectors for elements based on their interactive index from the browser state, including detection for elements within Shadow DOMs or iframes.

Beyond browser control, the [`create_namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L256) function integrates a [`file_system`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/registry/views.py#L171) object for managing virtual files, allowing LLMs to read from and write to a simulated file system. It also includes standard Python modules like [`json`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L30), [`asyncio`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L107), [`Path`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L316), [`csv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L1620), [`re`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L980), and [`datetime`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L6). For data analysis and manipulation, the namespace can optionally incorporate popular data science libraries such as [`numpy`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L64), [`pandas`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L64), [`matplotlib`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L64), [`BeautifulSoup`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/README.md?plain=1#L42), [`pypdf`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L42), and [`tabulate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L64), provided they are installed in the environment.

Additionally, the namespace includes a [`validate_task_completion`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L96) function, enabling the LLM to assess if a given task has been successfully accomplished. This self-assessment capability contributes to the LLM's iterative problem-solving and error correction. The [`done()`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/utils.py#L276) action is also included, with guardrails to ensure it is used as a final signal of task completion and not within conditional logic, guiding the LLM towards appropriate task finalization.

The [`create_namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L256) function dynamically populates this environment, making it adaptable to different project configurations and installed dependencies, thereby offering a flexible and powerful toolkit for AI-driven browser automation within a persistent Python execution context. The available tools and their usage can be documented dynamically using [`get_namespace_documentation`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L645) from [`browser_use/code_use/namespace.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py).

zoom\_in

The [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) orchestrates browser automation by integrating a Large Language Model (LLM) with a browser session and a Python execution environment, as defined in [`browser_use/code_use/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py). Its primary function is to execute complex tasks by iteratively asking an LLM for Python code, running that code, and providing feedback to the LLM about the current browser state.

The core of this orchestration is the [`run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L1073) loop, which manages the entire task execution process. This loop begins by initializing the browser, setting up the DOM service for interacting with web elements, and preparing a persistent Python [`namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L55). This namespace is crucial as it allows variables and functions to be maintained across multiple code execution steps, akin to a Jupyter notebook session. If the task specifies an initial URL, the agent navigates there first. The loop then proceeds by repeatedly requesting code from the LLM, executing it, and updating the agent's internal state.

When retrieving code from the LLM, the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) prepares a set of messages, including a textual representation of the current browser state and, optionally, a screenshot if vision capabilities are enabled. This rich context allows the LLM to make informed decisions about the next actions. The agent is designed to extract different types of code blocks from the LLM's response, handling Python code directly for execution and injecting other languages (like JavaScript or Bash) into the execution environment as needed. The agent also actively monitors for potential token limit issues with the LLM, providing signals to guide the LLM toward more efficient code generation. For more details on how the browser state is formatted for the LLM, refer to [LLM State Formatting and Prompting](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#code-execution-and-sandboxing-notebook-like-code-execution-for-llms-llm-state-formatting-and-prompting).

Code execution within the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) occurs in the persistent Python [`namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L55). This environment captures [`stdout`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L615) and [`stderr`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L645) to provide feedback on execution results. A notable feature is its ability to handle [`await`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L115) expressions directly, allowing for asynchronous browser interactions within the synchronous execution flow of the agent. This is achieved by wrapping the LLM-generated code in an [`async`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L111) function, preserving the variable scope across execution steps. The agent also includes mechanisms for handling various errors, such as JavaScript execution failures ([`EvaluateError`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L90)) or Python syntax errors, offering helpful diagnostics to the LLM.

To provide comprehensive feedback to the LLM, the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) continuously fetches the current browser state, which includes a concise textual description of the DOM and, if configured, a screenshot. This information is then used to update the LLM's understanding of the web page and its context.

Task completion is determined by a special flag set within the Python [`namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L55) by a [`done()`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/utils.py#L276) function, indicating that the LLM has successfully achieved the task's objective. The agent also incorporates mechanisms for error recovery, including detecting and managing consecutive execution errors or token limit issues, and attempts to guide the LLM towards resolving these problems. Throughout its operation, the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) records a detailed history of all steps, including LLM outputs, execution results, browser states, and token usage, which is valuable for evaluation and debugging.

The [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) also provides functionality to gracefully shut down the browser session if [`keep_alive`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L593) is not enabled, ensuring proper resource management. The complete execution history is made available externally through a [`history`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L84) property, which can be used by evaluation systems to assess the agent's performance.

The [`format_browser_state_for_llm`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/formatting.py#L12) function, found in [`browser_use/code_use/formatting.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/formatting.py), plays a key role in structuring the current state of a browser session into a coherent, readable format for a Large Language Model (LLM). This process is designed to give the LLM a clear picture of the web environment, enabling it to generate effective Python code for browser automation tasks. The formatted output provides essential context, including the current URL, page title, details on all open tabs, the page's scroll position, and any network requests that are still pending. Furthermore, it organizes and presents the variables available within the execution namespace, distinguishing between those created within specific code blocks and general variables, along with their data types and condensed string representations. A crucial component of this output is a compact, truncated representation of the Document Object Model (DOM), which is vital for the LLM to understand the webpage's structure and identify interactive elements.

In conjunction with this state formatting, the [`system_prompt.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L170) file, located at [`browser_use/code_use/system_prompt.md`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/system_prompt.md), outlines the agent's "Mental Model" and "STRATEGY," which are foundational for guiding the LLM's decision-making and code generation. This prompt establishes an iterative, notebook-like execution paradigm where the LLM produces and executes one Python code cell at a time. It explicitly details the asynchronous browser interaction functions available to the agent, such as [`navigate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L411), [`click`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L2122), [`input_text`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/infrastructure/test_registry_validation.py#L210), and [`evaluate`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1846) for executing JavaScript. A central design principle emphasized is the use of element indices for interactions, with the caveat that these indices are temporary and necessitate re-querying after any changes to the DOM. The prompt also lays out a structured strategy for accomplishing tasks, progressing from initial exploration and validation to batch processing and final verification, while stressing the importance of debugging and reviewing intermediate results. The [`done`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L2025) function is highlighted as the definitive step for marking task completion.

zoom\_in

The system manages and exports code execution sessions through several data models and utility functions, ensuring that the history of interactions and browser states can be reviewed, reproduced, or shared. The [`CodeCell`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L32) model in [`browser_use/code_use/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py) represents an individual unit of execution, encapsulating the code executed, its output, status (e.g., [`PENDING`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L26), [`SUCCESS`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L28), [`ERROR`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L22)), and the browser's state at that moment. These cells are aggregated within a [`NotebookSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L47) also defined in [`browser_use/code_use/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py), which maintains the overall sequence of actions, a persistent namespace for variables, and a comprehensive history of the agent's steps. The [`CodeAgentHistory`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L165) object stores a detailed record of each step, including LLM outputs, execution results, browser state snapshots, and performance metadata.

For external sharing or further analysis, sessions can be exported. The [`export_to_ipynb`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py#L12) function in [`browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py) converts a session into a Jupyter notebook file, which includes the setup for [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) and [`create_namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/namespace.py#L256) to make the notebook directly executable. This allows for a complete playback of the automation sequence within a standard notebook environment. Similarly, the [`session_to_python_script`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py#L177) function in [`browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/notebook_export.py) generates a standalone Python script, embedding the necessary browser control functions and the executed code cells into a single, runnable file. Both export mechanisms are designed to preserve embedded JavaScript logic and ensure the reproducibility of the automation tasks.

link

zoom\_in

The sandbox environment provides a mechanism for executing Python functions securely and remotely, particularly for browser automation tasks, by abstracting away the complexities of deployment and communication. It enables the execution of asynchronous Python functions within a managed environment, offering real-time feedback through event streaming and ensuring type-safe reconstruction of results.

The core of this functionality is provided by the [`sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L215) decorator, located in [`browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py). This decorator transforms an asynchronous Python function into one that can be executed remotely. It manages the capture of the function's code, its parameters (including explicit arguments and closure variables), and necessary imports. The decorator handles the serialization of this execution context, dynamically injecting only the required imports and variables into the remote environment to ensure a minimal and secure payload.

Communication with the remote sandbox is facilitated through Server-Sent Events (SSE). This streaming mechanism allows for real-time feedback during execution, including events related to browser creation, logs, instance readiness, and the final results or errors. The [`sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L215) decorator integrates various callbacks ([`on_browser_created`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/sandbox/example.py#L30), [`on_instance_ready`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L227), [`on_log`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L228), [`on_result`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L229), [`on_error`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L230)) that allow developers to react to these events.

The system also ensures the type-safe reconstruction of results from the remote execution. The [`_parse_with_type_annotation`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L533) function, found in [`browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py), is responsible for recursively reconstructing complex Python objects, such as Pydantic models, dataclasses, and Enums, from the sandbox's JSON output based on the decorated function's return type annotation. This process aims to ensure that the return value of the remote function accurately matches the expected local Python type.

The data models for SSE communication are defined in [`browser_use/sandbox/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py). These models, including [`SSEEventType`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L14), [`BrowserCreatedData`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L26), [`LogData`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L34), [`ResultData`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L50), and [`ErrorData`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L56), provide a structured and type-safe way to exchange information between the client and the sandbox. The [`SSEEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L64) class, which encapsulates the event type and its data, also includes methods like [`from_json`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L87) for parsing JSON strings into type-safe instances and type guard methods ([`is_browser_created`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L118), [`is_log`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L122), [`is_result`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L126), [`is_error`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L130)) for safely accessing event payloads. This structured approach to event handling enhances code readability and maintainability. For more details on the serialization and deserialization processes, refer to [Serialization and Deserialization of Remote Calls](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#code-execution-and-sandboxing-type-safe-sandboxed-remote-execution-serialization-and-deserialization-of-remote-calls). The implementation of Server-Sent Events is further elaborated in [Event Streaming via Server-Sent Events (SSE)](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#code-execution-and-sandboxing-type-safe-sandboxed-remote-execution-event-streaming-via-server-sent-events-sse).

zoom\_in

Remote function execution within the sandboxed environment relies on robust serialization and deserialization processes to transmit code and data between the client and the sandbox. The [`sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L215) decorator, defined in [`browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py), orchestrates this by capturing the function's source code, its parameters, and any referenced variables. This information is then packaged using [`cloudpickle`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L47), a library chosen for its ability to serialize complex Python objects, including closures, which standard [`json`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L30) or [`pickle`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L305) might struggle with. The serialized data is then [`base64`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L355) encoded for transmission.

Upon receiving the serialized payload, the sandbox environment reconstructs the execution context. After the remote function completes its execution, its return value is serialized back to the client. On the client side, the [`_parse_with_type_annotation`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L533) function in [`browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py) is responsible for deserializing this result. It reconstructs complex Python objects, such as Pydantic models, dataclasses, and Enums, based on the function's return type annotation. This reconstruction process occurs without triggering Pydantic or dataclass validation, assuming the sandbox's output conforms to the expected schema. This design ensures that the remote function's return value is accurately transformed back into the appropriate local Python type, facilitating seamless integration with the client's codebase. For further details on the remote execution environment, refer to [Type-Safe Sandboxed Remote Execution](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#code-execution-and-sandboxing-type-safe-sandboxed-remote-execution).
