zoom\_in

Server-Sent Events (SSE) provide a mechanism for real-time communication between the client and the sandbox environment. This system facilitates streaming events from the remote execution of Python functions, offering immediate feedback on browser creation, logs, execution results, and errors. The [`sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L215) decorator, detailed in [Type-Safe Sandboxed Remote Execution](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#code-execution-and-sandboxing-type-safe-sandboxed-remote-execution), orchestrates this communication.

The structure of these events is defined by models in [`browser_use/sandbox/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py). The [`SSEEventType`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L14) enum categorizes the various types of events, such as [`BROWSER_CREATED`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L17), [`LOG`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L20), [`RESULT`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L21), and [`ERROR`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L22). Each event type is associated with a specific data model (e.g., [`BrowserCreatedData`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L26) for browser creation events, [`LogData`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L34) for log messages, [`ExecutionResponse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L41) nested within [`ResultData`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L50) for execution outcomes, and [`ErrorData`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L56) for error reporting), ensuring that streamed data is well-structured and type-safe.

The [`SSEEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L64) class serves as a generic wrapper for all SSE messages, containing both the event type and its corresponding data payload. It includes a [`from_json`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L87) method to parse incoming JSON strings into strongly typed [`SSEEvent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L64) instances, leveraging the event type to correctly deserialize the associated data. This robust deserialization mechanism, coupled with type guard methods like [`is_browser_created`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L118) or [`is_log`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/views.py#L122), simplifies the processing of diverse event payloads by consumers of the SSE stream. This approach ensures that developers can reliably interact with the event's payload in a type-safe manner, enhancing code readability and maintainability.

zoom\_in

The processing of browser Document Object Model (DOM) data is central to interacting with and understanding web pages. This system transforms raw DOM snapshots from the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) into an enhanced, actionable representation. This representation includes crucial information such as element visibility, clickability, and layout details like bounding boxes. The core of this processing is handled in the [`browser_use/dom/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py) file, which orchestrates the retrieval and enhancement of DOM data from various CDP domains, including [`DOMSnapshot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L78), [`DOM`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L612), [`Accessibility`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L60), [`Page`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L39), and [`Runtime`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L166). The resulting [`EnhancedDOMTreeNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L373) objects, defined in [`browser_use/dom/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py), aggregate these diverse data points, providing a comprehensive view of each DOM element. This includes not only standard DOM properties but also accessibility information and snapshot data such as computed styles and paint order. The system also supports generating stable hashes for elements, crucial for tracking elements across page changes and enabling features like historical replay.

A key aspect of DOM processing is the optimized serialization of the enhanced DOM for consumption by Large Language Models (LLMs) and code agents. This serialization prioritizes token efficiency, interactivity, and structural relevance to effectively convey the state of the web page to AI models. Various serialization strategies are implemented in the [`browser_use/dom/serializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer) directory. For example, [`DOMEvalSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/eval_serializer.py#L112) in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/eval_serializer.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/eval_serializer.py) creates concise representations highlighting interactive elements, while [`DOMCodeAgentSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/code_use_serializer.py#L58) in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/code_use_serializer.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/code_use_serializer.py) focuses on minimal token usage for code agents. The process involves identifying clickable elements, as detailed in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/clickable_elements.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/clickable_elements.py), and filtering elements based on paint order to only represent visually present components, implemented in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/paint_order.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/paint_order.py).

Beyond structured DOM data, the system also extracts clean, structure-aware markdown from browser content. The [`browser_use/dom/markdown_extractor.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/markdown_extractor.py) module handles this process, converting HTML into markdown, preprocessing it to clean up artifacts like URL encoding and large JSON blobs, and then chunking the markdown based on semantic structure. This chunking algorithm aims to preserve context by, for instance, splitting at header blocks and providing overlap prefixes between chunks.

For interactive analysis and debugging of DOM extraction and processing, the [`browser_use/dom/playground`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/playground) directory offers interactive utilities. These tools, such as the one described in [`browser_use/dom/playground/extraction.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/playground/extraction.py), allow developers to navigate websites, click elements, type text, and analyze the extracted DOM state, including performance metrics. Furthermore, the system includes utilities for generating robust CSS selectors for DOM elements, as found in [`browser_use/dom/utils.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/utils.py). These selectors prioritize stable attributes and handle special characters to ensure reliable element identification even as web pages change dynamically. The [`browser_use/dom/playground/multi_act.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/playground/multi_act.py) file also demonstrates how an AI agent can automate web form completion tasks, leveraging these DOM processing capabilities.

### Enhanced DOM Data Processing and Representation

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) system transforms raw Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) DOM snapshots into an enhanced, actionable representation of a web page. This process integrates various data points from the browser to provide a comprehensive understanding of each DOM element's visibility, interactivity, and layout.

The core of this enhancement is performed within the [`DomService`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py#L35) class in [`browser_use/dom/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py). This service orchestrates the retrieval and merging of information from multiple CDP domains, including [`DOMSnapshot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L78), [`DOM`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L612), [`Accessibility`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L60), [`Page`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L39), and [`Runtime`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L166). It constructs an [`EnhancedDOMTreeNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L373) (defined in [`browser_use/dom/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py)) for each element, which aggregates standard DOM properties, accessibility information from the [`Accessibility`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L60) tree ([`EnhancedAXNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L310)), and layout and style data from the [`DOMSnapshot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L78) ([`EnhancedSnapshotNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L324)).

Key transformations and data augmentations performed during this process include:

-   **Visibility and Clickability Calculation**: Each [`EnhancedDOMTreeNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L373) is evaluated for its visibility and clickability. Visibility checks ([`is_element_visible_according_to_all_parents`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py#L242) in [`browser_use/dom/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py)) consider not only an element's own CSS properties (like [`display`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L548), [`visibility`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py#L84), [`opacity`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py#L85)) but also its position relative to the viewport of all containing iframes, accounting for scroll positions and a configurable [`viewport_threshold`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py#L54). Clickability is determined by analyzing JavaScript event listeners, HTML tags, ARIA roles, accessibility properties, and CSS cursor styles, as detailed in [CDP-Use: Low-Level Browser Interaction](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-cdp-use-low-level-browser-interaction) and the [`ClickableElementDetector`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/clickable_elements.py#L4) in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/clickable_elements.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/clickable_elements.py).
-   **Layout Information (Bounding Boxes)**: The system extracts [`DOMRect`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L279) bounding boxes (coordinates and dimensions) from the CDP snapshot data. These coordinates, initially in device pixels, are converted to CSS pixels using the [`device_pixel_ratio`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L201) to ensure consistent measurements, as handled by [`build_snapshot_lookup`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/enhanced_snapshot.py#L47) in [`browser_use/dom/enhanced_snapshot.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/enhanced_snapshot.py). This provides precise layout information for each element.
-   **Accessibility Data Integration**: The [`Accessibility`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L60) tree provides crucial semantic information about elements. This data, including roles, names, and states, is integrated into the [`EnhancedDOMTreeNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L373) as an [`EnhancedAXNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L310), enriching the element's description for AI agents and improving its discoverability.
-   **Computed Style Extraction**: Essential computed styles such as [`display`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L548), [`visibility`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py#L84), [`opacity`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py#L85), [`cursor`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/README.md?plain=1#L45), and [`overflow`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L457) are selectively extracted from the CDP snapshot. This selective extraction, managed by [`_parse_computed_styles`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/enhanced_snapshot.py#L38) in [`browser_use/dom/enhanced_snapshot.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/enhanced_snapshot.py), is a performance and stability optimization to avoid requesting excessive data from Chrome.
-   **Paint Order and Stacking Contexts**: Information about an element's paint order and stacking context is extracted from the [`DOMSnapshot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLAUDE.md?plain=1#L78). This data is critical for understanding which elements might visually obscure others, enabling more accurate visual processing and filtering of irrelevant elements for AI consumption. [`PaintOrderRemover`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/paint_order.py#L131) (discussed in [Optimized DOM Serialization for AI Agents](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#dom-processing-and-content-extraction-optimized-dom-serialization-for-ai-agents)) uses this information to mark visually obscured elements.
-   **Cross-Origin Iframe Handling**: The system can recursively fetch and enhance the DOM for cross-origin iframes, up to a configurable depth. This ensures a comprehensive view of complex web pages that embed content from different domains, with visibility and size checks to avoid processing tiny or off-screen iframes.
-   **Stable Hashing**: The [`EnhancedDOMTreeNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L373) includes multiple hashing mechanisms, such as [`compute_stable_hash`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L828) in [`browser_use/dom/views.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py). This function filters out dynamic CSS classes and focuses on static attributes to generate a more stable hash across browser sessions, which is crucial for reliably identifying elements for historical tracking and replay.

The resulting [`EnhancedDOMTreeNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L373) provides a rich, traversable representation of the web page, combining visual, structural, and semantic information into a single, cohesive object, ready for further processing by AI agents or for detailed analysis.

### Optimized DOM Serialization for AI Agents

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) framework employs sophisticated strategies for serializing enhanced DOM trees into various formats, specifically tailored for consumption by different AI agents, such as Large Language Models (LLMs) and code agents. This serialization process prioritizes token efficiency, the preservation of interactivity, and structural relevance to ensure that AI agents receive an optimal representation of the web page.

The overall DOM processing pipeline begins with transforming raw Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) DOM snapshot data into an enriched representation that includes element visibility, clickability, and layout information. This enhanced data, encapsulated in [`EnhancedDOMTreeNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L373) objects, serves as the foundation for all subsequent serialization. For more details on this initial processing step, see [Enhanced DOM Data Processing and Representation](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#dom-processing-and-content-extraction-enhanced-dom-data-processing-and-representation).

A central orchestrator for the serialization process is the [`DOMTreeSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/serializer.py#L41) class, defined in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/serializer.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/serializer.py). This class coordinates multiple steps to prepare the DOM for agent consumption. It begins by simplifying the enhanced DOM tree into [`SimplifiedNode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L218) objects, pruning non-content elements and handling special structures like iframes and shadow DOM. This simplification is critical for reducing noise and focusing on relevant elements.

A key aspect of this optimization involves filtering elements based on their visual presence and paint order. The [`PaintOrderRemover`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/paint_order.py#L131), found in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/paint_order.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/paint_order.py), identifies and marks nodes that are visually obscured by other elements with a higher paint order. This ensures that agents do not receive information about elements that are not visible to a human user, enhancing the relevance of the serialized output. The remover considers transparency and opacity when determining whether an element truly obscures another.

Another crucial step in preparing the DOM for AI agents is the detection of clickable and interactive elements. The [`ClickableElementDetector`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/clickable_elements.py#L4) in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/clickable_elements.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/clickable_elements.py) determines if a DOM node is interactive based on a comprehensive set of heuristics. These include JavaScript event listeners, HTML tags (e.g., [`button`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/events.py#L129), [`a`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/LICENSE#L5)), ARIA roles, accessibility properties, and CSS cursor styles. This information is vital for AI agents to understand which elements they can interact with.

For LLMs, a concise representation is paramount to manage token limits while retaining structural and interactive context. The [`DOMEvalSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/eval_serializer.py#L112), defined in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/eval_serializer.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/eval_serializer.py), generates a compact string that highlights interactive elements with specific notations (e.g., [`[i_X]`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/eval_serializer.py#L159) for unique identifiers). It prioritizes key HTML attributes relevant for query writing and form interaction, selectively includes semantic elements, and truncates less critical details like long attribute values or repetitive lists to optimize token usage.

Code agents, which directly execute Python code to interact with the browser, require an even more token-optimized serialization that provides just enough context for accurate script generation. The [`DOMCodeAgentSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/code_use_serializer.py#L58), located in [`browser_use/dom/serializer/code_use_serializer.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/code_use_serializer.py), focuses on minimal token usage while preserving essential interactive context. It achieves this by aggressively filtering attributes, only including a predefined set of crucial ones, and truncating all attribute values and inline text to very short lengths. It also collapses common container elements like [`div`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/README.md?plain=1#L7) and [`span`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/utils.py#L48) if they do not contribute significant unique context, effectively flattening the hierarchy to further save tokens.

After these various filtering and optimization steps, interactive elements are assigned unique identifiers, and the processed tree is converted into a structured string format. This final serialization considers various display options, including how to represent shadow DOM content and compound components within HTML elements (like input fields with increment/decrement buttons or select boxes with options). This multi-faceted approach ensures that [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) can provide AI agents with a highly relevant, token-efficient, and structurally accurate representation of web content, enabling effective browser automation.

The module [`browser_use/dom/markdown_extractor.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/markdown_extractor.py) processes browser content into clean, structured markdown. The [`extract_clean_markdown`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/markdown_extractor.py#L22) function takes content from either a [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) or a [`DomService`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/service.py#L35), converts it to HTML using an [`HTMLSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/serializer/html_serializer.py#L6) (described in [Optimized DOM Serialization for AI Agents](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#dom-processing-and-content-extraction-optimized-dom-serialization-for-ai-agents)), and then transforms the HTML into markdown. This process includes preprocessing steps to remove URL encoding artifacts, large JSON blobs, and excessive newlines, ensuring the markdown is clean and readable.

For managing large content, the module provides a structure-aware markdown chunking algorithm via the [`chunk_markdown_by_structure`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/markdown_extractor.py#L391) function. This algorithm first categorizes markdown lines into atomic blocks, such as headers, code fences, tables, lists, and paragraphs, identified by [`_BlockType`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/markdown_extractor.py#L181) and represented by [`_AtomicBlock`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/markdown_extractor.py#L191). It then greedily assmebles these blocks into chunks, prioritizing splitting at header blocks to maintain semantic coherence. To preserve context across chunks, especially for tables, it generates an [`overlap_prefix`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L925) that includes lines from the previous chunk or table headers. This approach ensures that agents consuming these chunks receive logically coherent segments of information, even when the original content is extensive.

### Interactive DOM Analysis and Automation Tools

link

The project provides interactive utilities within [`browser_use/dom/playground`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/playground) for testing, analyzing, and debugging the Document Object Model (DOM) extraction process from websites. The file [`browser_use/dom/playground/extraction.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/playground/extraction.py) includes an interactive tool that allows developers to navigate through various websites, extract and examine their DOM states, and simulate user interactions like clicks and text input. This tool also provides performance metrics, such as the time taken for DOM extraction and the token count of the generated user messages, which can be useful for optimizing agent prompts.

Additionally, the project offers tools for automated web form completion using an AI agent. The file [`browser_use/dom/playground/multi_act.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/playground/multi_act.py) demonstrates how an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) can be configured with a Large Language Model (LLM) and a [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) to autonomously navigate to a URL and complete web forms based on a specified task. This capability highlights the integration of AI decision-making with browser automation for multi-step tasks.

### Robust CSS Selector Generation

link

python

content\_copyCopy

def generate\_css\_selector\_for\_element(enhanced\_node) -> str | None: """Generate a CSS selector using node properties.""" \# Stub: Import re \# Stub: Validate enhanced\_node and tag\_name tag\_name = enhanced\_node.tag\_name.lower().strip() css\_selector = tag\_name \# Prioritize ID for most specific selection if enhanced\_node.attributes and 'id' in enhanced\_node.attributes: element\_id = enhanced\_node.attributes\['id'\].strip() \# Handle valid IDs directly if re.match(r'^\[a-zA-Z\]\[a-zA-Z0-9\_-\]\*$', element\_id): return f'#{element\_id}' \# Use attribute selector for IDs with special characters else: return f'{tag\_name}\[id="{element\_id.replace('"', '\\\\"')}"\]' \# Add class attributes if enhanced\_node.attributes and 'class' in enhanced\_node.attributes: \# Stub: Define valid\_class\_name\_pattern regex for class\_name in enhanced\_node.attributes\['class'\].split(): if class\_name.strip() and valid\_class\_name\_pattern.match(class\_name): css\_selector += f'.{class\_name}' \# Define a set of safe and useful attributes for selection SAFE\_ATTRIBUTES = { 'id', 'name', 'type', 'placeholder', 'aria-label', 'role', 'href', 'target', 'data-id', 'data-qa', 'data-cy', 'data-testid', \# Dynamic attributes } \# Add other safe attributes if enhanced\_node.attributes: for attribute, value in enhanced\_node.attributes.items(): if attribute == 'class' or attribute not in SAFE\_ATTRIBUTES: continue \# Handle empty values, special characters, and regular values if value == '': css\_selector += f'\[{attribute}\]' elif any(char in value for char in '"\\'<>\`\\n\\r\\t'): \# Stub: Collapse whitespace and escape quotes for complex values css\_selector += f'\[{attribute}\*="{safe\_value\_from\_collapsed}"\]' else: css\_selector += f'\[{attribute}\="{value}"\]' \# Stub: Final validation and fallback to tag\_name return css\_selector

The system includes a utility to generate robust and stable CSS selectors for elements within the Document Object Model (DOM). This functionality is crucial for reliably identifying and interacting with web page elements, even when aspects of the page's structure or styling change.

The [`generate_css_selector_for_element`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/utils.py#L8) function, found in [`browser_use/dom/utils.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/utils.py), is designed to create CSS selectors by prioritizing specific attributes to ensure stability. It first attempts to use an element's [`id`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L241) attribute, as IDs are typically unique and stable identifiers. If an ID is not suitable or available, it then incorporates class names. Finally, it considers a defined set of "safe" HTML and accessibility attributes, such as [`name`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/base.py#L27), [`type`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L1008), [`placeholder`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L1212), and [`aria-label`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/dom/views.py#L30), to form a more specific selector.

A key aspect of this utility is its handling of dynamic attributes and special characters. It filters out common dynamic class patterns that might change across page loads or user interactions, thereby improving the selector's resilience. Additionally, the function includes logic to properly escape special characters found in attribute values and IDs, preventing syntax errors and ensuring the generated selector is valid. This careful approach to attribute prioritization and character handling contributes to the reliability of element identification across different states or versions of a web page. For a more comprehensive understanding of how the DOM is processed, refer to [Enhanced DOM Data Processing and Representation](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#dom-processing-and-content-extraction-enhanced-dom-data-processing-and-representation).

link

zoom\_in

The virtual file system within [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) enables agents to manage in-memory file objects that are also persistently stored on disk. This system supports various file types, offering specialized read/write operations tailored to their formats. The [`browser_use/filesystem`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem) directory contains the core implementation of this file system.

The [`FileSystem`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L295) class, defined in [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py), acts as the central manager, maintaining both an in-memory representation of files and synchronizing their content with a dedicated directory on disk. This approach provides rapid access to file data while ensuring persistence. It leverages an abstract [`BaseFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L83) class, with concrete implementations for common file types such as [`MarkdownFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L142), [`TxtFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L150), and [`JsonFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L158). Specialized file types like [`PdfFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L182) and [`DocxFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L231) extend this base with custom [`sync_to_disk_sync`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L109) and [`sync_to_disk`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L113) methods, allowing the system to render their text content into the respective document formats. For instance, [`PdfFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L182) utilizes [`reportlab`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L43) to generate PDF documents from text content, while [`DocxFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L231) uses the [`docx`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L49) library. This design facilitates extensibility and encapsulates type-specific logic.

A key feature of the file system is its intelligent handling of PDF content. When dealing with large PDF files, the [`read_file_structured`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L448) method within [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py) employs an Inverse Document Frequency (IDF)-like scoring mechanism to identify and extract content from the most relevant pages, ensuring that agents receive concise and pertinent information. The file system also incorporates robust filename management, with utilities such as [`_is_valid_filename`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L349) and [`sanitize_filename`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L366) in [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py) to validate and clean filenames, preventing issues like path traversal and ensuring secure operations.

Beyond file system management, [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) integrates with external services, notably the Gmail API, to enhance agent capabilities. The [`browser_use/integrations/gmail`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail) directory manages this integration, enabling agents to read emails, extract verification codes, and retrieve general email content. The [`GmailService`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py#L25) class, found in [`browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py), handles the OAuth2 authentication flow, including token management (loading, refreshing, and saving tokens), to ensure persistent and secure access to the Gmail API. It also includes methods like [`get_recent_emails`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py#L51) to fetch and parse email data, making it available for agent consumption.

Agent actions related to email interaction, such as [`get_recent_emails`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py#L51), are defined in [`browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py) and registered via the [`register_gmail_actions`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py#L29) function in [`browser_use/integrations/gmail/__init__.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/__init__.py). These actions allow agents to programmatically interact with Gmail, supporting tasks like retrieving two-factor authentication (2FA) codes or monitoring specific email communications. The system supports both file-based and direct access token authentication for the Gmail API, providing flexibility for different deployment scenarios.

The virtual file system in [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) provides an in-memory representation of files that are asynchronously persisted to disk, ensuring that agents can quickly access and manipulate data while maintaining state across sessions. The core component of this system is the [`FileSystem`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L295) class, defined in [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py), which manages all file operations. It is responsible for initializing the file system within a specified base directory, and for mapping file extensions to their corresponding [`BaseFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L83) subclasses.

The [`BaseFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L83) class, an abstract blueprint for all file types also in [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py), defines common properties such as filename and content, and methods for reading, writing, and appending content. Each [`BaseFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L83) object stores its content in memory, allowing for rapid access and modification by the agent. Crucially, any changes to a [`BaseFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L83) object's content trigger an asynchronous write operation to disk. This is handled by [`sync_to_disk`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L113), which uses a thread pool executor to perform disk I/O without blocking the main event loop, ensuring that file operations do not impede the agent's responsiveness.

To maintain data integrity and allow for the restoration of the file system's state, [`FileSystem`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L295) supports serialization and deserialization. The [`get_state`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L834) method captures the current configuration of the file system, including all managed files and their contents, into a [`FileSystemState`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L287) object. Conversely, the [`from_state`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L849) class method can reconstruct a [`FileSystem`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L295) instance from a [`FileSystemState`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L287) object, effectively restoring all files to their previous states and re-persisting them to disk. This mechanism is vital for enabling agents to resume tasks across restarts or to maintain context over extended periods.

Beyond standard text files, the virtual file system also incorporates specialized handling for document types like PDFs and DOCX. For these files, the [`PdfFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L182) and [`DocxFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L231) subclasses, located in [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py), extend [`BaseFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L83) with custom methods for rendering their text content into the respective document formats on disk, utilizing external libraries. This specialized handling ensures that agents can interact with and generate complex document types as needed. The system also includes robust filename management, with utility functions like [`sanitize_filename`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L366) and [`_resolve_filename`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L393) to prevent issues such as path traversal and ensure that filenames conform to expected patterns.

The virtual file system in [`browser_use/filesystem`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem) supports specialized file types such as [`PdfFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L182) and [`DocxFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L231), which extend the [`BaseFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L83) abstraction to provide custom read/write capabilities beyond basic text handling. These specialized file types are designed to intelligently interact with complex document formats.

For instance, [`PdfFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L182) and [`DocxFile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L231) implement custom [`sync_to_disk_sync`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L109) and [`sync_to_disk`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L113) methods. These methods leverage external libraries—[`reportlab`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L43) for PDF generation and [`docx`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/pyproject.toml#L49) for Word documents—to render their in-memory text content into the respective document formats. This includes basic parsing of markdown-like headers within the content to structure the generated documents. To optimize resource usage, these external libraries are lazily imported only when their specific functionality is required, preventing unnecessary dependencies from being loaded.

A key feature for PDF files is the intelligent content extraction mechanism within the [`read_file_structured`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L448) method of the [`FileSystem`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L295) class in [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py). When dealing with large PDF documents, this mechanism employs an Inverse Document Frequency (IDF)-like scoring algorithm to prioritize and extract content from the most relevant pages. This ensures that when a PDF is too large to process in its entirety, the most distinctive and informative sections are retrieved, enhancing the efficiency of content summarization and analysis. The file system also supports standard text-based formats like Markdown, JSON, CSV, HTML, and XML, as well as image formats (JPG, JPEG, PNG) by converting them to base64 for structured output.

### Robust Filename Management and Validation

link

python

content\_copyCopy

class FileSystem: \# ... (other methods and initializations) ... def \_is\_valid\_filename(self, file\_name: str) -> bool: """Check if filename matches the required pattern: name.extension Allows letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, dots, parentheses, spaces, and Chinese characters in the name part, followed by a dot and a supported extension. """ \# Dynamically get supported extensions from registered file types extensions = '|'.join(self.\_file\_types.keys()) \# self.\_file\_types is a dict of extension -> FileClass \# Regex to validate filename format: \[name\_chars\].\[extension\] \# name\_chars: alphanumeric, underscores, hyphens, dots, parentheses, spaces, Chinese characters \# extension: any of the supported extensions pattern = rf'^\[a-zA-Z0-9\_\\-\\.\\(\\) \\u4e00-\\u9fff\]+\\.({extensions})$' base\_name = os.path.basename(file\_name) if not re.match(pattern, base\_name): return False \# Ensure the name part (before the last dot) is non-empty name\_part = base\_name.rsplit('.', 1)\[0\] return len(name\_part.strip()) > 0 \# ... (rest of the FileSystem class) ...

To ensure the integrity and security of file operations, the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) environment incorporates utility functions for robust filename management. These functions prevent common issues like path traversal and ensure that filenames conform to expected patterns.

The system validates filenames using [`_is_valid_filename`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L349) within [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py), which checks against a pattern allowing alphanumeric characters, underscores, hyphens, dots, parentheses, spaces, and Chinese characters, ensuring that they end with a supported extension. If a filename does not meet these criteria, a detailed error message is generated by [`_build_filename_error_message`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L38) to inform users of the specific reasons for invalidity, such as unsupported binary extensions or missing extensions.

For filenames that contain invalid characters but are otherwise structurally sound, the [`sanitize_filename`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L366) function, also in [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py), automatically cleans them. This process replaces problematic characters, collapses multiple hyphens, and removes leading or trailing hyphens and dots while preserving the file extension. Finally, the [`_resolve_filename`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L393) function, which is located in [`browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py), is responsible for resolving filenames. It sanitizes the name if necessary and normalizes it to its basename, effectively preventing directory traversal vulnerabilities by ensuring all file operations refer to files within the designated directory.

zoom\_in

The [`GmailService`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py#L25) class, primarily defined in [`browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py), centralizes the management of Gmail API interactions. This service is instrumental in enabling browser automation agents to interact with Gmail, performing tasks such as reading emails and extracting verification codes.

The [`GmailService`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py#L25) class handles authentication with the Gmail API using OAuth2. It supports both file-based authentication, where credentials and tokens are managed via specified file paths, and direct authentication using an [`access_token`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sync/auth.py#L202). The service also implements robust token management, automatically loading, refreshing, and saving tokens to ensure persistent authentication sessions. This design minimizes the need for repeated user interaction after initial authorization. The authentication process includes an [`InstalledAppFlow`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py#L16) for user consent, which opens a browser for the user to grant access.

The [`register_gmail_actions`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py#L29) function, found in [`browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py), integrates these Gmail functionalities into the agent's toolkit. It allows actions, such as [`get_recent_emails`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py#L51), to be registered and made available for agent use, abstracting the underlying API calls. This enables agents to query emails based on keywords and time filters, with the results formatted for easy consumption.

link

zoom\_in

Agent actions provide a programmatic way for AI agents to interact with external services, specifically Gmail in this context. The core mechanism involves registering these actions within a [`Tools`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L345) object, making them callable by an agent.

The [`get_recent_emails`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py#L51) action is a primary function available to agents, designed to retrieve recent email messages. When an agent invokes this action, it can specify search criteria using [`GetRecentEmailsParams`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py#L22). This parameter object allows for defining a [`keyword`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/actions.py#L25) to filter emails (e.g., 'otp' for one-time passwords, 'github' for notifications) and setting [`max_results`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/views.py#L28) to control the number of emails returned. Additionally, the action automatically applies a [`time_filter`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py#L139) of five minutes to focus on the most recent communications, ensuring that agents primarily process current information.

Before executing, the system ensures that the [`GmailService`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py#L25) is authenticated, either through a provided [`access_token`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sync/auth.py#L202) or by initiating an OAuth authentication flow if necessary. Once emails are retrieved, they are formatted into a readable string that includes essential details such as the sender, subject, date, and body content. This structured output is then provided to the agent for further processing or decision-making. The system handles cases where no emails match the criteria or errors occur during retrieval, logging issues and returning an informative message to the agent. This integration allows AI agents to perform tasks requiring email interaction, such as extracting verification codes for two-factor authentication or monitoring specific email alerts. Further details on the [`GmailService`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/integrations/gmail/service.py#L25) can be found in [Gmail API Integration and Authentication Flow](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#virtual-file-system-and-integrations-gmail-api-integration-and-authentication-flow).

zoom\_in

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) project integrates the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to enable communication between AI agents and browser automation capabilities. This allows [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) to act as both a client that can consume tools from external MCP servers and a server that exposes its own browser automation functionalities to other MCP clients. The core of this integration is managed within the [`browser_use/mcp`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp) directory.

As an MCP client, [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) uses the [`MCPClient`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/client.py#L47) (defined in [`browser_use/mcp/client.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/client.py)) to connect to external MCP servers. Once connected, it discovers the tools offered by these servers and dynamically registers them as callable actions within the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) environment. This process involves converting the JSON Schema definitions of MCP tool parameters into Pydantic models, ensuring type validation and clear parameter definitions for the newly integrated actions. This allows AI agents within [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) to leverage functionalities from various external MCP services as if they were native [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) actions. For more details on the client-side integration and action registration, refer to [MCP Client-Side Integration and Action Registration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#model-context-protocol-mcp-integration-mcp-client-side-integration-and-action-registration).

Conversely, [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) can also function as an MCP server through the [`BrowserUseServer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L187) (implemented in [`browser_use/mcp/server.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py)). This server exposes [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9)'s browser automation capabilities, such as navigating, clicking, typing, and managing tabs, as MCP tools. These tools are registered with their respective names, descriptions, and input schemas, allowing external MCP clients (including other AI agents or development environments) to control a web browser managed by [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9). The server also handles session management, including tracking and cleaning up expired browser sessions, and redirects all logging to [`sys.stderr`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L1586) to prevent interference with the MCP's JSON RPC communication on [`stdout`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L615). The [`main`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L2010) function in [`browser_use/mcp/__main__.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/__main__.py) serves as the entry point for running this MCP server directly. For further information on [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) as an MCP server, see [Browser-Use as an MCP Server](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#model-context-protocol-mcp-integration-browser-use-as-an-mcp-server).

The [`MCPToolWrapper`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/controller.py#L29) (found in [`browser_use/mcp/controller.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/controller.py)) plays a crucial role in bridging MCP tools with [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9)'s action system. It dynamically generates Pydantic models for tool parameters from JSON Schemas and wraps MCP tool calls into functions that fit [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9)'s action framework. This allows [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) to seamlessly integrate and execute external MCP tools while maintaining its internal structure for action handling. Detailed information about this can be found under [MCP Tool Wrapping and Dynamic Pydantic Model Generation](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#model-context-protocol-mcp-integration-mcp-tool-wrapping-and-dynamic-pydantic-model-generation).

zoom\_in

The [`MCPClient`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/client.py#L47) in [`browser_use/mcp/client.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/client.py) enables [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) to connect with external Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. This integration allows [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) to leverage functionalities exposed by other services as if they were native actions. The client establishes a connection to an MCP server, typically by spawning a process and communicating over standard I/O. Once connected, it queries the MCP server to discover available tools and their respective schemas.

A key aspect of this integration is the dynamic registration of these external MCP tools as callable actions within the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) framework. Each discovered MCP tool is wrapped into an asynchronous function, which is then registered in a [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) [`Registry`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/registry/service.py#L32) object. This mechanism ensures that [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agents can seamlessly interact with external MCP services. The client also manages the conversion of JSON Schema definitions for MCP tool parameters into Pydantic models. This dynamic model generation provides strong type validation and clear parameter definitions for the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) actions, facilitating robust and predictable interactions with external tools. The client also captures telemetry for connection, disconnection, and tool call events, providing insights into the usage and performance of these integrations. Further details on the overall MCP integration can be found in [Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#model-context-protocol-mcp-integration).

The [`BrowserUseServer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L187) component exposes [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9)'s browser automation capabilities to external clients via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). This allows various external systems, such as AI agents or development environments, to programmatically interact with a web browser managed by [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9). The server provides a standardized, language-agnostic interface for browser control, enabling a decoupled architecture where browser tasks can be initiated and monitored remotely.

The server's implementation, primarily found in [`browser_use/mcp/server.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py), initializes core [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) components such as [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132), [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94), [`Tools`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L345), [`ChatOpenAI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/openai/chat.py#L25), and [`FileSystem`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/filesystem/file_system.py#L295). These components collectively provide the necessary functionalities for executing browser operations and agent tasks. It registers a suite of browser automation features as MCP tools. These tools encompass actions like navigating to URLs, clicking elements, typing text, scrolling, managing browser tabs (listing, switching, closing), and handling entire browser sessions (listing, closing specific sessions, and cleaning up expired ones). The server also supports the execution of autonomous agent tasks, allowing an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) to utilize an LLM (either [`ChatOpenAI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/openai/chat.py#L25) or [`ChatAWSBedrock`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/aws/chat_bedrock.py#L22)) to perform complex, high-level tasks within the browser.

A critical design consideration for the [`BrowserUseServer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L187) is its logging configuration. To ensure the integrity of the JSON RPC communication over [`stdout`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L615), all server logs are aggressively redirected to [`sys.stderr`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L1586) and largely suppressed, as detailed in [`_configure_mcp_server_logging`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L61) and [`_ensure_all_loggers_use_stderr`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L103) within [`browser_use/mcp/server.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py). This prevents any log messages from interfering with the protocol's data stream. Additionally, the server includes mechanisms for tracking and cleaning up inactive browser sessions, preventing resource accumulation through a background cleanup task. The server's entry point for direct execution is provided by [`browser_use/mcp/__main__.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/__main__.py).

### MCP Tool Wrapping and Dynamic Pydantic Model Generation

link

The [`MCPToolWrapper`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/controller.py#L29) plays a crucial role in bridging external Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools with [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9)'s internal action system. It enables [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) to discover and dynamically incorporate functionalities offered by external MCP servers, treating them as native actions that can be invoked by agents or other components.

This wrapper, primarily implemented in [`browser_use/mcp/controller.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/controller.py), manages the connection to an MCP server, discovers its advertised tools, and registers these tools as callable actions within a [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) [`Registry`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/registry/service.py#L32). For each MCP tool, the [`MCPToolWrapper`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/controller.py#L29) dynamically generates a Pydantic model based on the tool's JSON Schema input definition. This ensures that when an MCP tool is called as a [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) action, its parameters are rigorously validated and typed, aligning with [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9)'s strong typing conventions.

The core of this integration is the [`mcp_action_wrapper`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/client.py#L309) function, dynamically created for each MCP tool. This wrapper handles the invocation of the remote MCP tool, filtering out any [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9)\-specific parameters that might be inadvertently passed, and converting the MCP tool's response into an [`ActionResult`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L312) compatible with the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) ecosystem. This process allows [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agents and workflows to seamlessly interact with capabilities provided by diverse MCP services, abstracting away the underlying communication protocol. The [`MCPToolWrapper`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/controller.py#L29) is initialized with a [`Registry`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/registry/service.py#L32) and the command-line arguments required to connect to the MCP server, and it leverages asynchronous operations to maintain non-blocking communication. For a detailed understanding of how [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) acts as an MCP client and integrates external tools, refer to [MCP Client-Side Integration and Action Registration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#model-context-protocol-mcp-integration-mcp-client-side-integration-and-action-registration).

## Command-Line Interface (CLI) for Browser Automation
