The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) command-line interface (CLI) provides a persistent and efficient way to automate web browser interactions. Its primary goal is to enable fast execution of browser commands by maintaining active browser sessions in the background. This architecture reduces latency and allows for complex, multi-step browser workflows to be executed seamlessly from the command line.

The CLI, primarily defined in [`browser_use/skill_cli`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli), acts as a client that communicates with a separate session server. This server, implemented in [`browser_use/skill_cli/server.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/server.py), manages instances of [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) and keeps them alive across commands. Communication between the client and server occurs via Inter-Process Communication (IPC), utilizing Unix sockets on non-Windows systems and TCP sockets on Windows, following a structured wire protocol defined in [`browser_use/skill_cli/protocol.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/protocol.py). This design ensures that the CLI client can start quickly, while computationally intensive browser operations are handled by the persistent server.

Core functionalities of the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) CLI include navigating web pages, interacting with elements, managing browser tabs, executing JavaScript, and extracting data. For instance, commands exist to [`open`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L429) URLs, [`click`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L2122) elements, [`type`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L1008) text into input fields, [`scroll`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1241) pages, and [`screenshot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L1387) content. The [`state`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L172) command is particularly useful, returning details about the current page, including interactive elements, which can then be targeted by index for subsequent actions. The CLI also provides robust cookie management, with commands to [`get`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/sessions.py#L61), [`set`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L323), [`clear`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/conftest.py#L234), [`export`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L19), and [`import`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L105) cookies, crucial for maintaining session persistence and authenticated access.

A significant feature of the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) CLI is its integration with Python. The [`browser-use python`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) command allows users to execute Python code within a persistent browser session. This Python environment, managed by [`PythonSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/python_session.py#L25) in [`browser_use/skill_cli/python_session.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/python_session.py), maintains a namespace across executions, enabling variables and imported modules to persist. It also exposes a [`browser`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/browser/save_cookies.py#L14) object, a synchronous wrapper around asynchronous browser operations, allowing users to script complex browser interactions directly within their Python code. For more details on the code execution environment, see [Notebook-Like Code Execution for LLMs](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#code-execution-and-sandboxing-notebook-like-code-execution-for-llms).

The CLI supports various browser modes to accommodate different use cases. Users can choose between [`chromium`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L279) (the default headless, isolated mode), [`--headed`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md?plain=1#L47) (a visible Chromium window), [`--browser real`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md?plain=1#L359) (which utilizes the user's local Chrome installation, including existing cookies and sessions), and [`--browser remote`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md?plain=1#L46) (for connecting to cloud-hosted browser instances). The [`profile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases/find_influencer_profiles.py#L25) subcommands allow for comprehensive management of both local Chrome profiles and cloud profiles, including the ability to synchronize local browser cookies to a cloud profile via [`browser-use profile sync`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L1950).

For advanced automation, the [`browser-use run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) command integrates with Large Language Models (LLMs) to enable AI agents to autonomously complete complex browser tasks. This capability often requires an API key, which is managed securely by the system as described in [API Key Management and Security](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#command-line-interface-cli-for-browser-automation-api-key-management-and-security).

The overall structure of the CLI commands is modular, with distinct handlers for agent tasks, browser control, Python execution, and session management located in [`browser_use/skill_cli/commands`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/commands). Utility functions in [`browser_use/skill_cli/utils.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/utils.py) handle platform-specific details like socket paths, PID file management, and locating browser executables, ensuring cross-platform compatibility.

### Architecture and Session Management

zoom\_in

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) CLI is designed around a client-server architecture to provide fast and persistent browser automation. This architecture ensures that a browser instance can remain active across multiple CLI commands, significantly reducing the overhead associated with launching a new browser for each operation.

The core of this system involves a client, primarily initiated by [`browser_use/skill_cli/main.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/main.py), which communicates with a persistent [`SessionServer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/server.py#L26). This server, implemented in [`browser_use/skill_cli/server.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/server.py), runs as a background process and is responsible for managing [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) instances. The client-server communication is handled through Inter-Process Communication (IPC) using Unix sockets on non-Windows systems and TCP sockets on Windows, adhering to a defined wire protocol specified in [`browser_use/skill_cli/protocol.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/protocol.py). This protocol uses JSON-RPC style [`Request`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/protocol.py#L12) and [`Response`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/protocol.py#L35) dataclasses for structured message exchange.

This design enables a responsive user experience by delegating resource-intensive tasks, such as browser initialization and lifecycle management, to the background server. When a user issues a command, the [`main.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/init_cmd.py#L344) client quickly dispatches it to the already-running [`SessionServer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/server.py#L26), which then executes the browser operation and returns the result. This approach minimizes latency for subsequent commands, as the browser state is maintained and ready for immediate interaction. The server also manages the startup and shutdown of [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) instances, ensuring proper resource allocation and cleanup.

Session persistence is a key aspect, allowing users to interact with the same browser instance across different CLI commands without needing to re-authenticate or re-navigate to specific pages. This is managed by the [`SessionRegistry`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/sessions.py#L25) in [`browser_use/skill_cli/sessions.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/sessions.py), which tracks and provides access to active [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) objects. Utility functions in [`browser_use/skill_cli/utils.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/utils.py) assist in managing the paths for these IPC sockets and process ID (PID) files, which are crucial for the client to locate and interact with the server.

The modular command structure, with various command handlers located in [`browser_use/skill_cli/commands`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/commands), further enhances maintainability. These handlers (for agent tasks, browser control, Python execution, and session management) receive commands from the [`SessionServer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/server.py#L26) and perform the actual operations on the managed [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94). For a deeper dive into the lifecycle and events within a browser session, refer to [Browser Session and Lifecycle Management](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-browser-session-and-lifecycle-management) and [Event System for Browser Communication](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-event-system-for-browser-communication).

### Browser Modes and Profile Management

zoom\_in

The command-line interface for [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) offers various browser modes to accommodate different automation needs, including local development, real-world browsing, and cloud-based execution. These modes dictate how browser instances are launched and managed, impacting persistence, authentication, and performance.

The default mode utilizes a headless Chromium browser, providing an isolated and efficient environment for automation tasks that do not require visual interaction or existing user data. For scenarios where visual interaction is needed, the [`--headed`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md?plain=1#L47) option can be used to launch a visible Chromium window.

For tasks requiring access to a user's existing browser data, such as authenticated sessions or stored cookies, [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) supports a "real" browser mode ([`--browser real`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md?plain=1#L359)). This mode connects to a local installation of the user's Chrome browser, allowing automation scripts to operate within the same context as a human user. This can be further specified by selecting a particular Chrome profile using [`--profile NAME`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md?plain=1#L185), enabling the management of distinct user identities or configurations. The system locates Chrome executables and profile paths using platform-specific logic, as defined within [`browser_use/skill_cli/utils.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/utils.py).

A "remote" browser mode ([`--browser remote`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md?plain=1#L46)) is available for cloud-based automation. This mode connects to a cloud-hosted browser instance, offering advantages such as scalability and execution in different geographical locations. This functionality requires an API key for authentication, which is managed through a dedicated system that prioritizes environment variables, configuration files, and interactive user input for secure storage and retrieval, as described in [`browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py).

A key feature related to profile management is the ability to synchronize cookies between local Chrome profiles and cloud profiles. This [`profile sync`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L1950) command facilitates the transfer of authenticated sessions from a user's local machine to a cloud environment, ensuring persistent access to services that require login. This process involves extracting cookies from a local profile and uploading them to a newly created cloud profile. This capability is managed through interactions with a [`Browser-Use Cloud API`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L1), leveraging internal Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) calls for precise cookie handling. Comprehensive commands are available for managing both local Chrome and cloud profiles, including listing, creating, getting, updating, and deleting them, as handled within [`browser_use/skill_cli/main.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/main.py). These profile management capabilities contribute to the overall [Command-Line Interface (CLI) for Browser Automation](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#command-line-interface-cli-for-browser-automation) and specifically support persistent browser sessions as detailed in [Architecture and Session Management](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#command-line-interface-cli-for-browser-automation-architecture-and-session-management).

zoom\_in

The [`browser-use python`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) command provides a Jupyter-like environment within a persistent browser session, enabling the execution of Python code to automate browser interactions. This environment maintains a consistent namespace across multiple commands, allowing variables and imported modules to persist, thus supporting iterative development and debugging directly from the command line.

The core component enabling this functionality is the [`PythonSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/python_session.py#L25) class, defined in [`browser_use/skill_cli/python_session.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/python_session.py). This class manages the persistent [`namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L55) where user-defined variables and browser objects reside. It also tracks the execution history and results of each code snippet. When Python code is executed, [`PythonSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/python_session.py#L25) injects a [`browser`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/browser/save_cookies.py#L14) object into this [`namespace`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/views.py#L55). This [`browser`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/browser/save_cookies.py#L14) object is an instance of [`BrowserWrapper`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/python_session.py#L111), which serves as a synchronous interface to the underlying asynchronous browser operations. This abstraction allows users to write straightforward, synchronous Python code to control the browser, while the [`BrowserWrapper`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/python_session.py#L111) handles the complexities of asynchronous communication with the [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94) on a separate event loop. For more details on the session server architecture, refer to [Architecture and Session Management](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#command-line-interface-cli-for-browser-automation-architecture-and-session-management).

Commands for interacting with this Python environment, such as executing code strings, running scripts from files, resetting the namespace, or listing variables, are handled by the [`handle`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/commands/agent.py#L13) function in [`browser_use/skill_cli/commands/python_exec.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/commands/python_exec.py). This handler ensures that Python code execution is performed in a separate thread to avoid blocking the main event loop, thereby maintaining responsiveness of the CLI while browser interactions and Python computations occur concurrently.

The [`browser-use run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) command orchestrates the execution of AI agents to autonomously complete complex browser tasks. This command leverages the core capabilities of the command-line interface (CLI) to control browser interactions while integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) for decision-making and action generation. The primary entry point for agent tasks is the [`handle`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/commands/agent.py#L13) function within [`browser_use/skill_cli/commands/agent.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/commands/agent.py).

Before an agent can execute, the system validates the presence of an API key, which is essential for accessing LLM services. This validation is performed using [`require_api_key`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py#L24). If an API key is not found, an [`APIKeyRequired`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py#L9) exception is raised, prompting the user for necessary credentials. For details on API key management, refer to [API Key Management and Security](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#command-line-interface-cli-for-browser-automation-api-key-management-and-security).

The [`handle`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/commands/agent.py#L13) function dynamically configures an LLM instance through [`get_llm`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L350). This utility prioritizes different LLM providers based on available environment variables, supporting [`ChatBrowserUse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/browser_use/chat.py#L31), [`ChatOpenAI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/openai/chat.py#L25), [`ChatAnthropic`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/anthropic/chat.py#L33), and [`ChatGoogleGenerativeAI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/commands/agent.py#L105). If specific environment variables like [`BROWSER_USE_API_KEY`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L19), [`OPENAI_API_KEY`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L127), [`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L131), or [`GOOGLE_API_KEY`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L135) are set, the corresponding LLM is configured. Otherwise, a default LLM is used.

Once an LLM is configured, an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) instance is created with the specified task, the chosen LLM, and the active [`browser_session`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/tests/ci/conftest.py#L158). The agent's [`run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L1073) method is then invoked, initiating the autonomous task execution within the browser. The system processes and formats the agent's final results, including the outcome, the number of steps taken, and a status indicating whether the task was completed. Error handling is integrated to catch and log exceptions that occur during agent execution, providing structured error responses.

### API Key Management and Security

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) CLI incorporates a system for managing API keys, which are essential for enabling advanced features such as LLM-powered data extraction and autonomous agent tasks. This system prioritizes security and user-friendliness by supporting multiple methods for API key retrieval and ensuring secure storage.

The core mechanism for obtaining an API key is through the [`require_api_key`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py#L24) function, defined in [`browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py). This function follows a hierarchical approach to locate the key:

1.  It first checks the [`BROWSER_USE_API_KEY`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L19) environment variable.
2.  If not found in the environment, it then attempts to read the key from a [`config.json`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L96) file, which is located in a platform-specific configuration directory (e.g., [`~/.config/browser-use/config.json`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/skills/browser-use/SKILL.md?plain=1#L316) on Linux/macOS). The [`get_config_path`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/utils.py#L151) utility in [`browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py) determines this path consistently across operating systems.
3.  If the key is still not found and the execution environment is interactive, the system prompts the user to enter the API key. The [`prompt_for_api_key`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py#L72) function manages this interactive input, offering the option to save the key for future use.

For secure storage, the [`save_api_key`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py#L103) function, also in [`browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py), persists the provided API key to the [`config.json`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L96) file. A critical security measure is the setting of file permissions to [`0o600`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sync/auth.py#L81) (owner read/write only), which restricts access to the stored key.

If an API key is required but cannot be obtained through any of these methods, the system raises an [`APIKeyRequired`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py#L9) exception, providing a clear message to the user on how to resolve the issue. The [`get_api_key`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py#L122) function in [`browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py) offers an alternative that returns [`None`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L328) instead of raising an exception, useful for scenarios where the API key is optional.

This robust API key management system, detailed in [`browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/api_key.py), is integral for various CLI operations, including the [`browser-use run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) command which orchestrates agent tasks and LLM configuration. See [Agent Tasks and LLM Integration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#command-line-interface-cli-for-browser-automation-agent-tasks-and-llm-integration) for more details. The [`main`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L2010) function of the CLI, in [`browser_use/skill_cli/main.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/skill_cli/main.py), also incorporates API key validation, especially when operating in [`remote`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L695) browser mode. For general architecture and session management, refer to [Architecture and Session Management](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#command-line-interface-cli-for-browser-automation-architecture-and-session-management).

## Examples and Use Cases

link

Category

Description

Path

**Ad-Use**

Automates the creation of Instagram image ads and TikTok video ads from any landing page.

`examples/apps/ad-use`

**Msg-Use**

AI-powered message scheduler for WhatsApp, composing and scheduling personalized messages.

`examples/apps/msg-use`

**News-Use**

Monitors news websites, extracts articles, and performs sentiment analysis.

`examples/apps/news-use`

**Cloud Examples**

Demonstrates the usage of the Browser Use Cloud API with various features like fast mode, structured output, and proxy usage.

`examples/cloud`

**User Interfaces**

Showcases different user interfaces available for browser-use applications, including command-line, Gradio, and Streamlit.

`examples/ui`

The [`examples`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples) directory provides a comprehensive collection of demonstrations showcasing the capabilities of the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) library. These examples illustrate diverse applications of web automation with AI agents, ranging from basic web interactions to complex, multi-step processes and integrations with external services. The directory is structured to highlight different facets of the library's functionality, including various browser configurations, AI model integrations, and custom automation flows.

A common pattern across these examples involves the use of the [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) class, which serves as the central orchestrator for web automation tasks. The [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) is typically configured with a natural language task description and a Large Language Model (LLM) to interpret instructions and generate actions. Asynchronous programming with [`asyncio`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L107) is widely used to efficiently handle I/O-bound web operations. Environment variables are managed using [`load_dotenv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L106) for secure configuration of API keys and other sensitive data.

The examples demonstrate a variety of use cases:

**Practical Applications with AI Models** Real-world applications are showcased, leveraging [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agents with Google's generative AI models (Gemini, Veo). These include automating tasks such as Instagram and TikTok ad generation from landing page URLs, AI-powered message scheduling and sending via WhatsApp Web, and monitoring news websites to extract, summarize, and analyze articles for sentiment. These applications illustrate the library's capability to build end-to-end automated workflows involving web interaction and advanced AI processing. See [`examples/apps`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/apps) for more details.

**Diverse Browser Configurations and Integrations** The examples demonstrate various ways to configure and interact with web browsers. This includes utilizing cloud-based browsers, running multiple automation tasks in parallel, and integrating with other browser automation tools like Playwright via the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). It also covers interactions with local Chrome browsers and managing browser states, such as saving and loading cookies. This flexibility allows [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) to adapt to different execution environments and integration needs. Examples for these configurations can be found in [`examples/browser`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/browser).

**Interacting with the Browser Use Cloud API** This set of examples focuses on integrating with the Browser Use Cloud API for executing web automation tasks. It covers fundamental operations such as creating and monitoring automation tasks, extracting structured data using Pydantic models, and leveraging "fast mode" for optimized execution speed and cost efficiency. Additionally, it demonstrates the use of country-specific proxies for accessing geo-restricted content and utilizing the Search API for real-time web content extraction. Refer to [`examples/cloud`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/cloud) for further information.

**Advanced Web Automation with CodeAgent** The [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) is demonstrated for handling complex web interaction tasks. This includes multi-page data extraction from e-commerce sites, where the agent navigates through various pages to collect product details. Another example involves filtering large datasets, such as the WebVoyager dataset, based on dynamic criteria like dates. These examples highlight the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54)'s ability to execute dynamically generated Python code within a persistent, Jupyter-like environment, enabling sophisticated data manipulation and web interaction. The implementations are available in [`examples/code_agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/code_agent).

**Extending Agent Functionality with Custom Tools** The library's extensibility is demonstrated through examples of developing and registering custom tools to enhance the [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132)'s capabilities. This includes automating two-factor authentication (2FA) using both Time-based One-Time Passwords (TOTP) and 1Password integration. Other custom tools enable features like applying action filters to control agent behavior based on web page domains, performing LLM-driven element interactions for intelligent clicks, custom web searches, file uploads, and sending email notifications upon task completion. The framework also supports orchestrating multiple agents in parallel for complex subtasks. These custom functions allow developers to tailor the [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) to specific operational requirements. See [`examples/custom-functions`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions) for more details.

**Core Agent and Browser Feature Demonstrations** A collection of examples illustrates specific features of the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) library. This includes various ways to configure agents, such as extending system prompts with custom instructions, handling follow-up tasks, and defining initial actions. Browser management features are also covered, including blocking or allowing specific domains, managing multiple browser tabs, implementing scrolling functionalities, and recording browser sessions as videos. Furthermore, examples show how to handle data and interact with LLMs, such as adding image context to agents, extracting structured data using Pydantic models, automating file downloads, and configuring fallback LLMs for improved resilience. Evaluation examples demonstrate how to assess an agent's performance against ground truth data using a judge LLM. Refer to [`examples/features`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features) for further details.

**AI Agents with File System Interaction** The library's capability to integrate web interactions with file system operations is demonstrated. Examples include extracting data from online PDFs and saving it to local files, generating CSV files from web-scraped data, and executing multi-step tasks that combine web navigation with local file manipulation (e.g., creating and modifying markdown files). These examples show how agents can read from and write to a dedicated virtual file system, providing a robust environment for data processing. The examples can be found in [`examples/file_system`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/file_system).

**Getting Started with Browser Use** Introductory examples simplify the onboarding process, covering core [`browser_use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L105) functionalities. These include basic web searching, programmatic form filling, structured data extraction from websites, and executing multi-step tasks. Optimizations for agent performance are also shown, such as using faster LLMs and configuring browser profiles for reduced wait times. These examples provide a foundational understanding of how to initiate and manage basic web automation tasks. See [`examples/getting_started`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/getting_started) for more information.

**Integrations with External Services** The library's integration capabilities with popular external services are highlighted. This includes examples for email integration, particularly for handling 2FA, where agents can read incoming emails to retrieve verification codes. Integrations with communication platforms like Discord and Slack enable AI agents to respond to commands received from these platforms and automate tasks involving them, effectively extending the reach of [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) into collaborative environments. Further details are available in [`examples/integrations`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/integrations).

**Integrating Various Large Language Models (LLMs)** A wide array of LLMs from different providers is integrated with [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agents for diverse web automation, search, and data extraction tasks. This includes examples for Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Cerebras, DeepSeek, Google Gemini, Groq, Mistral, OCI, Ollama, OpenAI, OpenRouter, and Vercel AI Gateway. The demonstrations highlight how different LLM capabilities can be leveraged to drive agent behavior and task execution. These integrations showcase the flexibility in choosing the most suitable LLM for specific automation needs. Refer to [`examples/models`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models) for these examples.

**Observability with Traceloop** The integration of [`Traceloop`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/observability/openLLMetry.py#L8) for enhancing observability during [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agent execution is demonstrated. This provides insights into the agent's autonomous operations, helping developers understand and debug the agent's decision-making process and execution flow. The example can be found in [`examples/observability`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/observability).

**Sandboxed Browser Automation** Examples demonstrate executing browser automation tasks within a sandboxed environment. This involves using the [`@sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L179) decorator to manage the execution environment and leveraging structured output, often with Pydantic models, for precise data extraction. The sandboxing mechanism ensures isolated and secure execution of tasks, while structured output facilitates reliable data processing. More information is available in [`examples/sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/sandbox).

**User Interfaces for Agent Interaction** Various user interfaces for interacting with [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agents are provided. This includes command-line interfaces for text-based interaction, as well as web-based interfaces built with Gradio and Streamlit. These examples allow users to define tasks and control agents through different interactive mediums, catering to various preferences and deployment scenarios. See [`examples/ui`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/ui) for these demonstrations.

**Real-World Use Cases and Scenarios** A collection of practical, real-world scenarios automated by [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) AI agents is presented. These include tasks such as applying for jobs, automating grocery shopping, solving CAPTCHAs, checking appointments, extracting content from PDFs, finding influencer profiles, and performing secure logins with 1Password. These examples underscore the library's utility in automating a wide range of everyday and business-critical web-based processes. Refer to [`examples/use-cases`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases) for these practical examples.

zoom\_in

This section showcases practical applications leveraging [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agents in conjunction with Google's generative AI models (Gemini, Veo). These examples demonstrate how AI can automate complex web-based tasks, including ad generation, message scheduling, and news monitoring with integrated analysis.

For instance, the ad generation application, found in [`examples/apps/ad-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/apps/ad-use), automates the creation of Instagram image ads and TikTok video ads directly from a landing page URL. An [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) analyzes the landing page to extract key information, which is then used by an [`AdGenerator`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/apps/ad-use/ad_generator.py#L103) to prompt Google's AI models, such as [`gemini-2.5-flash-image-preview`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/vercel/chat.py#L69) for image generation and [`veo-3.0-generate-001`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/apps/ad-use/ad_generator.py#L228) for video content. This application supports parallel ad generation for efficiency, storing all generated assets in a designated output directory.

The message scheduling application, located in [`examples/apps/msg-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/apps/msg-use), provides AI-powered message scheduling and sending via WhatsApp Web. The system handles the WhatsApp Web login process and then uses a [`ChatGoogle`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/google/chat.py#L46) model to interpret natural language instructions from a text file, allowing users to schedule and send messages. It also includes functionality for automated responses to unread messages, demonstrating dynamic interaction with web applications based on AI interpretation.

Finally, the news monitoring application within [`examples/apps/news-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/apps/news-use) continuously tracks news websites. An [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) navigates to specified URLs to extract the latest articles, including their titles, URLs, posting times, and content. The extracted articles are then processed by a [`ChatGoogle`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/google/chat.py#L46) model (Gemini Flash) to generate short and long summaries, as well as sentiment analysis. The system incorporates deduplication using MD5 hashing to prevent reprocessing the same articles and stores the analyzed news data persistently. This application can operate in either a single-run or continuous monitoring mode, offering flexible news intelligence gathering.

### Diverse Browser Configurations and Integrations

link

zoom\_in

The system supports various browser configurations, ranging from cloud-based instances to local browser control and direct interaction with the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). This flexibility allows for diverse automation scenarios and integration with existing tools.

For cloud-based operations, the system can launch and manage browser instances in the cloud. This is demonstrated by examples that use [`Browser(use_cloud=True)`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L29) to execute tasks remotely. Such setups often involve configuring proxy settings, as seen with [`cloud_proxy_country_code`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L226), to access geo-restricted content or simulate different user locations.

Parallel execution of browser tasks is also supported. Multiple browser instances and AI agents can operate concurrently using asynchronous programming constructs like [`asyncio.gather`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L1602). Each instance typically maintains an isolated [`user_data_dir`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L190) to prevent interference between parallel tasks, allowing for scalable automation efforts.

The system integrates with Playwright, enabling a combined approach where both [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) and Playwright interact with a single Chrome instance via CDP. This allows for leveraging Playwright's capabilities (e.g., form filling, screenshot capture, text retrieval) through custom actions registered within the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agent framework. For instance, [`playwright_fill_form`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/browser/playwright_integration.py#L154), [`playwright_screenshot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/browser/playwright_integration.py#L216), and [`playwright_get_text`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/browser/playwright_integration.py#L249) can be registered as actions. This integration often requires launching Chrome with a remote debugging port and connecting both frameworks to it.

For local browser interactions, the system can control an installed Chrome browser on the user's machine. This is configured by specifying the [`executable_path`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L381) to the Chrome binary and a [`user_data_dir`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L190) for profile management.

The system also provides mechanisms for managing browser state, such as cookies and local storage. By connecting to an existing Chrome instance, it can export the browser's storage state to a JSON file, which can then be reused to maintain session persistence. This is achieved by invoking [`export_storage_state`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L1170) on a configured [`Browser`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/__init__.py#L54) instance.

Direct interaction with the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) is possible, allowing low-level control over browser behavior. This is fundamental to how [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) operates, and it can be explicitly configured to connect to an external CDP endpoint. This approach allows fine-grained control over browser actions, which can be particularly useful for debugging or highly customized automation tasks. These various configurations allow developers to tailor the browser environment to the specific needs of their automation tasks, whether for scalability, specific integrations, or local development. For more details on low-level browser interaction, refer to [CDP-Use: Low-Level Browser Interaction](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core-cdp-use-low-level-browser-interaction).

### Interacting with the Browser Use Cloud API

link

python

content\_copyCopy

\# Create a task, wait for completion, and display results \# Instructions for the agent task\_description = ( "Go to DuckDuckGo and search for 'browser automation tools'. " "Tell me the top 3 results with their titles and URLs." ) \# This function creates a task via the API task\_id = create\_task(task\_description) \# Stubbed: see source for full implementation \# This function polls the API until the task is finished result = wait\_for\_completion(task\_id) \# Stubbed: see source for full implementation print('\\n📊 Results:') print('-' \* 30) print(f'Status: {result\["status"\]}') print(f'Steps taken: {len(result.get("steps", \[\]))}') if result.get('output'): print(f'Output: {result\["output"\]}') \# Shareable URLs for viewing the execution if result.get('public\_share\_url'): print(f'\\n🌐 Share URL: {result\["public\_share\_url"\]}')

The Browser Use Cloud API enables programmatic interaction with the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) platform for various browser automation tasks. This includes basic task management, structured data extraction, optimized automation modes, and proxy utilization for geo-specific content. The examples for these functionalities are located in the [`examples/cloud`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/cloud) directory.

The API supports the creation and monitoring of automation tasks. Tasks can be initiated with natural language instructions, and their status and detailed results can be retrieved and tracked until completion. For scenarios requiring high efficiency and cost-effectiveness, a "fast mode" is available. This mode leverages optimized language models and browser configurations to accelerate task execution.

Structured data extraction from websites is also a key feature. The API allows for the definition of data schemas using Pydantic models, ensuring that extracted content conforms to a specified structure. This structured output is then validated and parsed from the API responses.

For accessing geo-restricted content or verifying IP locations, the API integrates proxy usage. Tasks can be configured with country-specific proxies to simulate access from different geographical regions. Additionally, a dedicated Search API provides functionalities for real-time web content extraction, supporting both general Google searches across multiple sites and specific content retrieval from a given URL.

Throughout these interactions, robust API calls are maintained through retry mechanisms, such as [`_request_with_retry`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/cloud/01_basic_task.py#L35), which handle transient network issues with exponential backoff. Configuration of API keys and other sensitive parameters is managed securely using environment variables.

### Advanced Web Automation with CodeAgent

link

python

content\_copyCopy

async def main(): task = "Browse example.com and extract the main heading." \# Initialize the CodeAgent with a task and maximum steps. agent = CodeAgent( task=task, max\_steps=10, \# Limiting steps for a simple task ) try: \# Run the agent to execute the task. print('Running CodeAgent...') session = await agent.run() \# The 'session' object can be further inspected for results \# or exported, similar to how it's used in the examples. finally: \# Ensure the agent's browser resources are properly closed. await agent.close() if \_\_name\_\_ == '\_\_main\_\_': asyncio.run(main())

The [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) facilitates advanced web automation by allowing Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate and execute Python code within a persistent browser environment. This capability enables the handling of complex, multi-step web interactions that go beyond simple navigation or data extraction.

For instance, the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) can be used for multi-page data extraction tasks, such as gathering product details from e-commerce websites. An example demonstrates extracting product URL, name, price, and discount information from a site like Flipkart, traversing multiple pages to collect comprehensive data. This is achieved by initializing the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) with a clear task description and a maximum number of steps, allowing the LLM to dynamically write Python code to navigate, extract, and paginate as needed.

Another application involves filtering large datasets based on dynamic criteria. The [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) can be tasked with finding and downloading datasets, such as the WebVoyager dataset, and then processing them to remove entries that meet specific conditions, like tasks with dates older than the current date. This highlights the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54)'s ability to integrate web interaction (downloading a dataset) with local data manipulation through Python code execution.

The orchestration of these tasks typically involves initializing [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54) from the [`browser_use.code_use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/__init__.py#L55) module with a natural language [`task`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/secure.py#L64) and [`max_steps`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L270). The [`run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L1073) method then executes the task asynchronously, and the [`close`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L922) method is used for resource cleanup. The [`Laminar.initialize()`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/bu_oss.py#L16) method is called to set up the necessary environment for the [`CodeAgent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/code_use/service.py#L54)'s operation. These examples are found in the [`examples/code_agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/code_agent) directory, with specific implementations in [`examples/code_agent/extract_products.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/code_agent/extract_products.py) and [`examples/code_agent/filter_webvoyager_dataset.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/code_agent/filter_webvoyager_dataset.py). For further details on the underlying Python code execution environment, refer to [Notebook-Like Code Execution for LLMs](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#code-execution-and-sandboxing-notebook-like-code-execution-for-llms).
