The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) library allows for extending an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132)'s capabilities by registering custom tools, enabling it to perform tasks beyond its default actions. These custom functions, defined in Python, can be made available to the agent's underlying Large Language Model (LLM) as callable actions, expanding its operational scope.

For example, custom tools can automate two-factor authentication (2FA). This can involve either generating Time-based One-Time Passwords (TOTP) from a secret key, as shown in [`examples/custom-functions/2fa.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/2fa.py), or integrating with password managers like 1Password to retrieve 2FA codes, as demonstrated in [`examples/custom-functions/onepassword_2fa.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/onepassword_2fa.py). Such tools often include domain-specific restrictions to ensure they are invoked only on appropriate websites.

Custom tools can also replace or augment an agent's default functionalities. For instance, the default web search can be overridden by a custom tool that integrates with external search APIs like [`google.serper.dev`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/advanced_search.py#L42), allowing for more controlled or specialized search queries. This is illustrated in [`examples/custom-functions/advanced_search.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/advanced_search.py), where structured Pydantic models are used to define the expected output, ensuring the LLM's responses adhere to a specific schema.

For browser interactions, tools can provide intelligent element identification and manipulation. An example in [`examples/custom-functions/actor_use.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/actor_use.py) shows how an LLM can be leveraged to identify and click a "submit" button without relying on rigid CSS selectors.

File system interactions and data management are another area where custom tools enhance agent capabilities. The [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) can be equipped with actions to upload files to web pages, managing file paths and events internally, as seen in [`examples/custom-functions/file_upload.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/file_upload.py). Similarly, custom tools can be developed to save structured data, defined by Pydantic schemas, to local files, as shown in [`examples/custom-functions/save_to_file_hugging_face.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/save_to_file_hugging_face.py).

Beyond single agent tasks, custom tools can facilitate the orchestration of multiple agents. An example in [`examples/custom-functions/parallel_agents.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/parallel_agents.py) demonstrates how a main LLM can decompose a complex task into subtasks, each executed by a dedicated [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) instance in parallel. This approach leverages [`asyncio`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L107) to manage concurrent execution, often creating isolated browser profiles for each sub-agent to prevent interference.

Integrations with external services, such as email for notifications upon task completion, can also be implemented as custom tools. This allows agents to perform actions like sending emails using libraries such as [`yagmail`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/notification.py#L18), as shown in [`examples/custom-functions/notification.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/notification.py). For more on external integrations, refer to [Integrations with External Services](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#examples-and-use-cases-integrations-with-external-services).

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) framework also provides mechanisms for controlling agent behavior through action filters, as described in [`examples/custom-functions/action_filters.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/action_filters.py). These filters can restrict actions based on domain or implement conditional logic, such as using CSS injection to enhance visibility or preventing actions on specific page types.

The process of registering these custom functions involves decorating them as actions using [`@tools.registry.action`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/custom-functions/cua.py#L182) or [`@tools.action`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L783). These decorators allow for specifying parameters, describing the action for the LLM, and defining execution contexts, such as applicable domains or required inputs. The agent's [`Tools`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/tools/service.py#L345) object aggregates these registered actions, making them available for the LLM to choose and execute during its task-solving process.

### Core Agent and Browser Feature Demonstrations

link

python

content\_copyCopy

\# Assuming 'Agent' and 'Browser' are imported and dotenv is loaded async def main(): \# Initialize a browser session that stays open browser = Browser(keep\_alive=True) await browser.start() \# Create an agent for an initial task agent = Agent(task='search for browser-use.', browser\_session=browser) await agent.run(max\_steps=2) \# Run initial task \# Add a follow-up task to the same agent and browser session agent.add\_new\_task('return the title of first result') await agent.run() \# Agent executes the follow-up task await browser.kill() \# For an interactive follow-up scenario async def interactive\_follow\_up(): \# Assuming 'BrowserProfile' is imported and initialized profile = BrowserProfile(keep\_alive=True) \# Persistent browser profile agent = Agent(task="Go to reddit.com", browser\_profile=profile) await agent.run(max\_steps=1) while True: user\_response = input('\\n👤 New task or "q" to quit: ') if user\_response.lower() == 'q': break \# Add new task to the existing agent agent.add\_new\_task(f'New task: {user\_response}') await agent.run()

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) library provides a range of features for configuring AI agents and managing browser interactions, alongside tools for data handling and evaluation. These functionalities are demonstrated through various examples in the [`examples/features`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features) directory.

Agent configuration allows for tailoring the agent's behavior to specific tasks. This includes modifying its system prompt to inject custom instructions, as shown in [`examples/features/custom_system_prompt.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/custom_system_prompt.py). Agents can also be configured to perform a series of sequential tasks within a persistent browser session, a concept illustrated in [`examples/features/follow_up_task.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/follow_up_task.py) and [`examples/features/follow_up_tasks.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/follow_up_tasks.py). The execution can be initiated with predefined actions, such as navigating to specific URLs at startup, as detailed in [`examples/features/initial_actions.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/initial_actions.py). For complex workflows, multiple agents can operate concurrently within a single browser session, a pattern demonstrated in [`examples/features/parallel_agents.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/parallel_agents.py). The system also supports external control over an agent's execution by allowing registration of a callback function that can signal it to stop, as presented in [`examples/features/stop_externally.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/stop_externally.py).

Advanced browser management features enable fine-grained control over the browser environment. Navigation can be restricted to or from specific domains, preventing agents from accessing undesired content or limiting their scope, as shown in [`examples/features/blocked_domains.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/blocked_domains.py) and [`examples/features/restrict_urls.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/restrict_urls.py). The library supports multi-tab browsing, allowing agents to manage and interact with several browser tabs simultaneously, as exemplified in [`examples/features/multi_tab.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/multi_tab.py). For visual debugging and analysis, browser sessions can be recorded as videos, with the configuration for this feature available in [`examples/features/video_recording.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/video_recording.py).

In terms of Large Language Model (LLM) data handling, the system facilitates incorporating visual context into an agent's understanding by converting images into a format suitable for LLM processing. This is useful for tasks requiring visual interpretation, and its implementation is shown in [`examples/features/add_image_context.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/add_image_context.py). The library also supports structured data extraction from web content, where agents can be configured to output data conforming to predefined schemas using Pydantic models, as demonstrated in [`examples/features/custom_output.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/custom_output.py). For handling file downloads, agents can be directed to save files to a specified path, as outlined in [`examples/features/download_file.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/download_file.py). To enhance robustness, fallback LLMs can be configured to take over if the primary LLM encounters errors, ensuring task continuity, as seen in [`examples/features/fallback_model.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/fallback_model.py). The system also includes mechanisms for securely handling sensitive data by preventing its exposure to the LLMs, which is crucial for privacy and security, as shown in [`examples/features/secure.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/secure.py) and [`examples/features/sensitive_data.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/sensitive_data.py). Furthermore, different LLMs can be utilized for various stages of an agent's operation, such as using a smaller, faster model for page content extraction while retaining a larger model for overall planning, thereby optimizing resource usage, as demonstrated in [`examples/features/small_model_for_extraction.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/small_model_for_extraction.py).

For evaluating agent performance, tools are available to assess an agent's actions against a predefined ground truth. This includes configuring a separate LLM to act as a judge, which can provide insights into the agent's effectiveness and adherence to the task, as illustrated in [`examples/features/judge_trace.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/judge_trace.py). Additionally, the execution history of an agent can be saved, processed, and replayed, with options for re-evaluating steps and generating summaries, which aids in debugging and refining agent behavior. This is explored in [`examples/features/rerun_history.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/rerun_history.py).

### AI Agents with File System Interaction

link

python

content\_copyCopy

\# Create a directory for the agent's file system agent\_dir = pathlib.Path('./file\_system\_example') agent\_dir.mkdir(exist\_ok=True) task = """ Go to https://mertunsall.github.io/posts/post1.html Save the title of the article in "data.md" Then, use append\_file to add the first sentence of the article to "data.md" Finally, share the file with me. """ agent = Agent( task=task, llm=ChatOpenAI(model='gpt-4.1-mini'), \# Replace with your preferred LLM file\_system\_path=str(agent\_dir / 'fs'), \# Agent's working directory ) async def main(): await agent.run() \# The 'fs' directory within agent\_dir will contain the created 'data.md' \# In a real application, you might inspect or process this file. if \_\_name\_\_ == '\_\_main\_\_': asyncio.run(main())

[`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) instances can interact with web resources and perform file system operations. For example, an agent can be configured to navigate to a PDF URL, read its content, extract specific data points, and save them to a local file. Similarly, an agent can find current stock prices for specified companies from web pages and then generate a CSV file containing company names and their stock prices. An agent can also perform multi-step tasks involving web navigation, information extraction (such as title and first sentence from an article), creation and manipulation of a local markdown file, and reporting the outcome.

The agents are initialized with a specific task, a language model (LLM), and a dedicated [`file_system_path`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L569) for isolated storage of temporary files and outputs. This ensures a clean and managed environment for file operations. Asynchronous execution is leveraged for efficiency, particularly for I/O-bound web interactions. Environment variables are used to securely configure API keys for LLMs. The system also supports logging agent interactions and conversation history. These capabilities are demonstrated in examples such as [`examples/file_system/alphabet_earnings.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/file_system/alphabet_earnings.py), [`examples/file_system/excel_sheet.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/file_system/excel_sheet.py), and [`examples/file_system/file_system.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/file_system/file_system.py). For further examples, refer to [Examples and Use Cases](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#examples-and-use-cases).

### Getting Started with Browser Use

link

python

content\_copyCopy

\# Initialize the ChatBrowserUse LLM llm = ChatBrowserUse(model='bu-2-0') \# Define the search task task = "Search Google for 'what is browser automation' and tell me the top 3 results" \# Create an Agent with the task and LLM agent = Agent(task=task, llm=llm) \# Run the agent to execute the search await agent.run() \# The full script includes setup for API keys and asyncio execution. \# See the source file for complete details.

This section introduces core functionalities of the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) library, demonstrating how an AI [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) can automate web interactions based on natural language instructions. The examples provided cover fundamental use cases, such as basic web searching, programmatic form filling, and structured data extraction. These functionalities are orchestrated asynchronously using [`asyncio`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L107) to manage web-based operations efficiently.

The [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) is typically initialized with a natural language [`task`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/secure.py#L64) that outlines its objective and an [`llm`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L279) (Large Language Model) that interprets these instructions and guides the agent's actions. For instance, a basic web search task, demonstrated in [`examples/getting_started/01_basic_search.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/getting_started/01_basic_search.py), involves setting up an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) with a language model, like [`ChatBrowserUse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/browser_use/chat.py#L31), to perform a specified search.

Programmatic form filling, as shown in [`examples/getting_started/02_form_filling.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/getting_started/02_form_filling.py), illustrates how an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) can navigate to a URL, identify and interact with multiple form fields, and submit the form, all based on a detailed task description. Similarly, structured data extraction is exemplified in [`examples/getting_started/03_data_extraction.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/getting_started/03_data_extraction.py), where an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) extracts specific information, such as quotes, authors, and tags, from a website.

For more complex scenarios, multi-step tasks can be executed, as detailed in [`examples/getting_started/04_multi_step_task.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/getting_started/04_multi_step_task.py). This involves an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) performing a series of actions, such as conducting Google searches, identifying relevant articles, extracting information, visiting external pages, and summarizing findings.

Optimizing agent performance for speed-critical tasks is also addressed. The example in [`examples/getting_started/05_fast_agent.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/getting_started/05_fast_agent.py) demonstrates how to configure an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) with a fast LLM, a custom [`BrowserProfile`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/profile.py#L538) with minimal wait times, and a [`flash_mode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L72) setting to accelerate execution. This setup also includes an extended system message to guide the LLM toward faster operations.

Across these introductory examples, environment variables are loaded via [`load_dotenv()`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L109) for secure configuration, ensuring that sensitive data like API keys are handled appropriately. The [`agent.run()`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L115) method serves as the primary way to initiate and execute the [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132)'s defined task.

zoom\_in

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) library integrates with external communication platforms such as email, Discord, and Slack, enabling AI agents to automate tasks that are either triggered by these services or require interaction with them. This allows for scenarios where agents can receive instructions from a chat platform or handle multi-factor authentication codes from an email.

For email integration, the library provides mechanisms to manage and interact with email accounts, particularly for tasks like two-factor authentication (2FA). This involves setting up and validating OAuth credentials, retrieving codes from incoming emails, and securely handling email content. An [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) can be configured with specific tools to programmatically access email inboxes, read messages, and extract relevant information, such as 2FA codes. A dedicated [`EmailTools`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/integrations/agentmail/email_tools.py#L22) class, for instance, encapsulates email management functions for agent use, including waiting for new messages via websocket connections. Furthermore, specific implementations, like the [`GmailGrantManager`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/integrations/gmail_2fa_integration.py#L36), handle the OAuth authentication flow for Google Gmail, ensuring secure and authorized access.

The integration with Discord allows browser automation tasks to be initiated through Discord messages. A [`DiscordBot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/integrations/discord/discord_api.py#L18) listens for commands, extracts the task description, and then dispatches it to an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) for execution within a [`BrowserSession`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/browser/session.py#L94). The bot processes messages, orchestrates the agent's actions in the browser, and sends the results or status updates back to the Discord channel, effectively bridging chat commands with web automation.

Similarly, the Slack integration enables users to trigger browser automation tasks via Slack messages. A [`SlackBot`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/integrations/slack/slack_api.py#L28) is configured to handle incoming Slack events, processing user messages that contain specific commands. Upon receiving a command, the bot extracts the task and instructs an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) to perform the corresponding web automation. Updates and outcomes from the agent's actions are then communicated back to the Slack channel. This integration often leverages a [`FastAPI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/integrations/slack/slack_api.py#L12) application to handle incoming POST requests from Slack, ensuring secure and authenticated communication.

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) library provides extensive integration with various Large Language Models (LLMs) to drive its web automation agents. This enables agents to perform tasks such as web searching, data extraction, and general browser interaction by interpreting natural language instructions and generating appropriate actions. The [`examples/models`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models) directory showcases these integrations, illustrating how to configure [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) instances with different LLM providers and models.

A core component is the [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) class, which orchestrates web interactions. It is initialized with a natural language [`task`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/secure.py#L64) and an [`llm`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L279) instance. The [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) then uses the LLM to understand the task and decide on a sequence of browser actions to achieve the goal. Parameters such as [`max_steps`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L270), [`flash_mode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L72), and [`use_vision`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L271) can be configured to control the agent's behavior and performance.

The examples demonstrate integrations with a wide range of LLM providers:

-   **AWS Bedrock**: Integration with [`ChatAnthropicBedrock`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/aws/chat_anthropic.py#L32) and [`ChatAWSBedrock`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/aws/chat_bedrock.py#L22) allows agents to leverage models like Claude for tasks such as retrieving AWS pricing information or popular GitHub repositories.
-   **Azure OpenAI**: The [`ChatAzureOpenAI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/azure/chat.py#L34) class facilitates tasks like finding the cheapest flights on Google Flights.
-   **Cerebras**: [`ChatCerebras`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/cerebras/chat.py#L28) enables fast inference and direct text generation for web interaction tasks.
-   **Anthropic**: Models like [`claude-sonnet-4-0`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L163) can be used via [`ChatAnthropic`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/anthropic/chat.py#L33) for tasks like searching e-commerce sites and extracting prices.
-   **DeepSeek**: [`ChatDeepSeek`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/deepseek/chat.py#L29) supports web interaction, particularly through Google search, by allowing custom system messages to guide its behavior.
-   **Google Gemini**: [`ChatGoogle`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/google/chat.py#L46) integrates with various Gemini models, including [`gemini-3-pro-preview`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/gemini-3.py#L19) and [`gemini-flash-latest`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L384), to perform tasks like querying GitHub repository stars, often utilizing [`flash_mode`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/views.py#L72) for optimized performance.
-   **OpenAI**: [`ChatOpenAI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/openai/chat.py#L25) supports models such as [`gpt-4.1-mini`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L379) and [`gpt-5-mini`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/cli.py#L384) for diverse web automation, from navigating e-commerce sites to answering factual questions.
-   **Groq**: [`ChatGroq`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/groq/chat.py#L56) integrates with models like [`meta-llama/llama-4-maverick-17b-128e-instruct`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docs/supported-models.mdx#L274) for rapid responses in tasks like Amazon product searches.
-   **Mistral**: [`ChatMistral`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/mistral/chat.py#L25) with models like [`mistral-small-2506`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/mistral.py#L16) can be used for general question-answering tasks within a browser context.
-   **Other Integrations**: The library also provides examples for integrating with ModelScope, Moonshot, Novita.ai, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) via [`ChatOCIRaw`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/oci_raw/chat.py#L36), Ollama for local LLM execution, OpenRouter, Qwen via [`ChatOpenAI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/openai/chat.py#L25) (specifically enabling vision capabilities), and Vercel AI Gateway through [`ChatVercel`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/vercel/chat.py#L168).

A general [`ChatBrowserUse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/browser_use/chat.py#L31) class is available, typically configured with models like [`bu-2-0`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/simple.py#L15) or [`browser-use/bu-30b-a3b-preview`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/bu_oss.py#L22), to perform tasks such as comparing GitHub star counts.

The system supports "lazy import" of LLMs, as shown in [`examples/models/lazy_import.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/lazy_import.py), which allows dynamic access to models and simplifies switching between providers.

For developers working with Langchain, a compatibility layer is provided within the [`examples/models/langchain`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/langchain) subdirectory. [`ChatLangchain`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/langchain/chat.py#L20) acts as an adapter, wrapping [`LangChain`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/README.md?plain=1#L21) chat models to conform to the [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) [`BaseChatModel`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/base.py#L18) protocol. This includes a [`LangChainMessageSerializer`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/langchain/serializer.py#L28) that converts [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) message types into their [`Langchain`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/docs/supported-models.mdx#L337) equivalents, ensuring seamless integration.

Beyond specific LLM integrations, the examples demonstrate how agents can leverage pre-defined skills, identified by UUIDs, to execute specialized tasks. This modular approach allows for flexible and extensible agent capabilities.

Across all these examples, common design patterns include the extensive use of [`asyncio`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L107) for handling asynchronous operations, secure management of API keys via environment variables and [`load_dotenv`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L106), and a modular [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) architecture that leverages various LLM implementations for decision-making and task execution.

### Observability with Traceloop

link

python

content\_copyCopy

\# Load API key from environment variables api\_key = os.getenv('TRACELOOP\_API\_KEY') \# Initialize Traceloop SDK with the API key Traceloop.init(api\_key=api\_key, disable\_batch=True) async def main(): \# Create an Agent instance and run it await Agent('Find the founders of browser-use').run() if \_\_name\_\_ == '\_\_main\_\_': asyncio.run(main())

Integration of the [`Traceloop`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/observability/openLLMetry.py#L8) SDK with [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) provides a mechanism for enhancing observability during the execution of autonomous agents. The [`Traceloop`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/observability/openLLMetry.py#L8) SDK is initialized to ensure its availability and to facilitate the collection of telemetry data from the agent's operations. This setup allows for insights into the agent's decision-making process and execution flow as it navigates and interacts with web environments. The API key for [`Traceloop`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/observability/openLLMetry.py#L8) is loaded from environment variables, promoting secure configuration practices. An example of this integration is demonstrated in [`examples/observability/openLLMetry.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/observability/openLLMetry.py), where an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) is tasked with a specific web automation objective and its execution is monitored by [`Traceloop`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/observability/openLLMetry.py#L8). The agent's [`run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L1073) method is executed asynchronously, reflecting the typical operation of such agents.

### Sandboxed Browser Automation

link

python

content\_copyCopy

\# Define a Pydantic model for structured output (from structured\_output.py) class IPLocation(BaseModel): ip\_address: str = Field(description='The public IP address') country: str = Field(description='Country name') \# ... other fields \# Callback for browser creation (from example.py) def on\_browser\_ready(data): print(f'Live view: {data.live\_url}') @sandbox( log\_level='INFO', on\_browser\_created=on\_browser\_ready, \# server\_url='http://localhost:8080/sandbox-stream', ) async def get\_ip\_and\_location(browser: Browser): agent = Agent( task='Go to ipinfo.io and extract my IP address and location details', browser=browser, llm=ChatBrowserUse(model='bu-2-0'), output\_model\_schema=IPLocation, \# Configure for structured output ) result = await agent.run(max\_steps=10) return result.get\_structured\_output(IPLocation) async def main(): location = await get\_ip\_and\_location() if location: print(f'IP Address: {location.ip\_address}') print(f'Country: {location.country}')

Browser automation tasks can be executed within a sandboxed environment to ensure isolation and controlled resource usage. This is facilitated by the [`@sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L179) decorator, which manages the lifecycle of a browser environment for an asynchronous function. This decorator allows for the configuration of various parameters, such as logging levels and cloud-specific settings like [`server_url`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/sandbox/example.py#L31), [`cloud_profile_id`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L186), [`cloud_proxy_country_code`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L226), and [`cloud_timeout`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L713).

The [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) class, which is central to defining and executing browser automation tasks, integrates with this sandboxed approach. It takes a natural language prompt or task, a browser instance, and a Large Language Model (LLM) as input to perform its operations. The [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132)'s [`run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/mcp/server.py#L1073) method executes the task within the sandboxed browser.

Monitoring and callbacks are supported within this sandboxed execution. For instance, a function can be registered as a callback to the [`on_browser_created`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/sandbox/example.py#L30) parameter of the [`sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/sandbox/sandbox.py#L215) decorator, providing details such as [`session_id`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/actor/page.py#L73) and [`live_url`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/sandbox/example.py#L24) for monitoring the browser session.

For extracting structured data, the sandboxed environment can be configured to use Pydantic [`BaseModel`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L13) classes. The [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) can be initialized with an [`output_model_schema`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L292) to guide its output parsing, allowing for the retrieval of validated, structured results. This ensures that data extracted during automation tasks conforms to predefined schemas, facilitating further processing and integration. For more details on outputting structured data, see [Unified LLM Interface and Core Components](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#ai-agent-integration-and-orchestration-unified-llm-interface-and-core-components).

These sandboxed operations are typically executed asynchronously using [`async`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L111) functions and [`asyncio.run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L118), which is common for I/O-bound web interactions. The integration of LLMs, such as [`ChatBrowserUse`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/browser_use/chat.py#L31), allows the [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) to interpret instructions and generate actions within the sandboxed browser.

Examples demonstrating these capabilities can be found in [`examples/sandbox`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/sandbox), where they illustrate basic tasks like checking an IP address and extracting location information. These examples also highlight the use of environment variables like [`BROWSER_USE_API_KEY`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L19) for secure API authentication.

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) library provides various user interfaces to facilitate interaction with its AI agents for browser automation tasks. These interfaces demonstrate how to integrate the core agent functionality into different user experiences, enabling users to define tasks and control agents through command-line, web-based (Gradio), and Streamlit applications.

A command-line interface (CLI) is implemented in [`examples/ui/command_line.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/ui/command_line.py). This terminal-based tool allows users to specify tasks and select an Large Language Model (LLM) provider (e.g., OpenAI or Anthropic) via command-line arguments. The CLI dynamically loads the appropriate LLM based on user input and environment variables and then initializes an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) to perform the specified task. This provides a direct and scriptable way to interact with the automation agents.

For web-based graphical user interfaces, the project offers examples using Gradio and Streamlit. The Gradio interface, defined in [`examples/ui/gradio_demo.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/ui/gradio_demo.py), provides an interactive web application where users can input task descriptions, API keys, and model choices. The [`run_browser_task`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/ui/gradio_demo.py#L58) function orchestrates agent execution within this interface, allowing users to trigger automation tasks through a web browser. Similarly, the Streamlit interface, found in [`examples/ui/streamlit_demo.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/ui/streamlit_demo.py), offers another web-based approach. It allows users to input a task query and select an LLM provider through a web form, with agent execution managed by the [`run_agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/ui/streamlit_demo.py#L80) function. Both Gradio and Streamlit examples demonstrate how to embed [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agents into more user-friendly, interactive web applications.

Across all these UI examples, the pattern involves initializing an [`Agent`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/agent/service.py#L132) with a natural language [`task`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/features/secure.py#L64) and an appropriate [`llm`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/config.py#L279) (such as [`ChatAnthropic`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/anthropic/chat.py#L33) or [`ChatOpenAI`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/browser_use/llm/openai/chat.py#L25)). The agents are configured with features like vision capabilities ([`use_vision=True`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/qwen.py#L22)) and limits on actions per step ([`max_actions_per_step=1`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/models/qwen.py#L22)). The core asynchronous execution of tasks is managed by [`asyncio.run`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L118), which is standard for [`asyncio`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/AGENTS.md?plain=1#L107) applications. Environment variables are utilized for secure management of API keys and other configurations, ensuring that sensitive information is not directly exposed in the code. These diverse interfaces illustrate the flexibility of integrating [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) agents into various operational environments. For more information on agent capabilities, refer to [AI Agent Integration and Orchestration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#ai-agent-integration-and-orchestration).

### Real-World Use Cases and Scenarios

link

The [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) library provides capabilities for automating a wide array of web-based scenarios, enabling AI agents to interact with websites and perform tasks that typically require human intervention. These functionalities are demonstrated through various examples illustrating how agents can navigate web pages, input data, extract information, and manage complex workflows.

For instance, the system can automate job application processes, where an agent navigates to job portals, fills in personal information, uploads resumes (see [`examples/use-cases/apply_to_job.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases/apply_to_job.py)), and submits applications. Similarly, it supports automated grocery shopping workflows, allowing agents to search for items, add them to a cart, handle substitutions for unavailable items, and manage delivery options, as shown in [`examples/use-cases/buy_groceries.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases/buy_groceries.py) and [`examples/use-cases/shopping.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases/shopping.py). These examples often leverage Pydantic models for structured output, ensuring that extracted data conforms to predefined schemas.

Another practical application involves solving CAPTCHAs, where an agent can be guided to interact with a CAPTCHA challenge on a webpage and provide the correct response ([`examples/use-cases/captcha.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases/captcha.py)). The system can also be used to monitor and check for specific information on websites, such as available visa appointment slots, by using vision capabilities to interpret the webpage content ([`examples/use-cases/check_appointment.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases/check_appointment.py)).

Content extraction extends beyond standard web pages to PDF documents. Agents can navigate to URLs hosting PDFs, read their content, and extract specific data points, saving them to local files ([`examples/use-cases/extract_pdf_content.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases/extract_pdf_content.py)). For discovering information across the web, agents can be configured to find influencer profiles by performing web searches and extracting structured social media data ([`examples/use-cases/find_influencer_profiles.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases/find_influencer_profiles.py)).

Secure interactions are also addressed, particularly for login processes. The system can integrate with password managers like 1Password to securely log into websites, using custom actions to blur sensitive page content and inject credentials without exposing them to the underlying LLM ([`examples/use-cases/onepassword.py`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/examples/use-cases/onepassword.py)).

These examples demonstrate the flexibility of [`browser-use`](https://github.com/browser-use/browser-use/blob/c011d07e7e9e41c06b5c72036c6e3efa5b9f287d/CLOUD.md?plain=1#L9) in combining browser automation with AI decision-making to tackle a wide range of real-world scenarios. For more on how custom actions are integrated, refer to [Extending Agent Functionality with Custom Tools](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#examples-and-use-cases-extending-agent-functionality-with-custom-tools). The architecture that supports these capabilities is detailed in [Browser Automation Core](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#browser-automation-core) and [AI Agent Integration and Orchestration](https://codewiki.google/github.com/browser-use/browser-use#ai-agent-integration-and-orchestration).
